repositories.brief_summary: A week of targeted fixes to the pretty-printer, build system, runtime GC refactoring, and stdlib documentation cleanup across 14 commits.
repositories.new_features_summary: A new C API function `caml_c_thread_register_in_domain` was merged, allowing C threads to register into a specific OCaml domain rather than always domain 0.
repositories.new_features:
- [#14275](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14275) by [Jack Joergensen](https://github.com/jacknojo): **`caml_c_thread_register_in_domain`** added to the C API, enabling C threads created outside the OCaml runtime to register themselves into a chosen domain by passing the domain's unique ID, rather than being forced into domain 0 via `caml_c_thread_register`.
- [#14797](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14797) by [chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy): **Fixed `pprintast` to preserve attributes on package types**, avoiding silent attribute loss when pretty-printing `(module M with type t = ...)` expressions in surface syntax; backported to 5.5 branch.
- [#14802](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14802) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27): **Fixed detection of argument defaults in `configure`**, correcting a false error message that appeared when installing OCaml via opam on OpenSUSE with the `site-config` package present (follow-up to [#14760](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14760), [#14761](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14761)).
repositories.activity_summary: Nine PRs were merged covering the GC ephemeron refactor, configure fixes, pprintast corrections, documentation cleanup, and CI dependency updates.
repositories.activity:
- [#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808) opened by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA): **Minor build fixes for cross-compilation** including `.gitignore` entry for `yacc/ocamlyacc.opt` and assembly-related fixes for building against `*-pc-windows` targets with Wine or clang-cl.
- [#14804](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14804) opened by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27): **Expose `caml_ba_compare` and related bigarray internals** currently hidden behind `CAML_INTERNALS`, to allow external code to construct `custom_operations` structs for custom bigarray wrappers.
- [#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803) opened by [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch): **Allow using constructors as builder functions** by auto-eta-expanding argument-less constructors when a function type is expected (e.g. `List.map Some [1;2;3]`), under discussion with [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) who raised the alternative of restricting [#9005](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/9005) to unary constructors only.
- [#14801](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14801) opened by [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue): **Add level to abbreviations in `Texpand`** to address a typing internals issue; under review by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- [#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): **Refactored ephemeron phase-change logic in `major_gc.c`**, extracting `prepare_for_ephe_sweeping` and `global_prepare_for_ephe_{marking,sweeping}` helpers; behavior-neutral cleanup in preparation for GC pacing work.
- [#14722](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14722) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): **Continued fixing orphaning of ephemerons** in `runtime/major_gc.c`, addressing a bug found while studying [#14349](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14349).
- [#14810](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14810) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): **Removed an outdated comment from `stdlib/lazy.mli`** warning about ill-founded recursive definitions under `-rectypes` that no longer applied following [#14805](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14805).
- [#14811](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14811) by [Ronan Desplanques](https://github.com/ronan-d): **Fixed an identifier mismatch in the `Ptyp_constr` docstring** in `parsing/parsetree.mli` where the constructor parameter was documented as `lident` instead of the correct `tconstr`.
- [#14806](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14806) by [vip892766gma](https://github.com/vip892766gma): **Fixed duplicated "the" typos** in `typing/typecore.ml` and `typing/errortrace_report.ml` comments, reviewed by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb).
- [#14786](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14786) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA): **Updated GitHub Actions dependencies** (`actions/cache`, `actions/upload-artifact`, `actions/download-artifact`) in the TSan workflow; reviewed by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27).
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion centred on the constructor auto-eta-expansion proposal, active review of the Weak iteration PR, and an ongoing riscv64 jump-table miscompilation report.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803) by [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch): **Constructor auto-eta-expansion** when used as builder functions (e.g. `List.map Some`) is under discussion; [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) noted that the proposal makes constructor interpretation type-directed and suggested narrowing [#9005](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/9005) to unary constructors as a less invasive alternative; a developer meeting discussion was proposed.
- [#14792](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14792) by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots): **Add `Weak.{iter, iteri, foldi_left}`** accumulated 24 comments from [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb), [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls), and [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli), discussing API design trade-offs around allocation avoidance in `foldi_left` vs. the ergonomics of `iteri`.
- [#14799](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14799) by [nickbetteridge](https://github.com/nickbetteridge): **riscv64 jump-table miscompilation** when `-march` includes compressed instructions (e.g. `rv64gc`) — the assembler can compress 4-byte `j` slots to 2-byte `c.j`, breaking dispatch; 13 comments exploring the issue and possible fixes.
- [#14809](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14809) by [chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy): **Pretty-printing bug with `Ptyp_functor`** in OCaml 5.5 beta — inline extensions in the `Ptyp_functor` grammar are not reflected in the constructed AST, reported as a follow-on to the package-type attribute fix in [#14797](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14797).
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on GC correctness around ephemerons, active exploration of ergonomic stdlib additions, and attention to cross-compilation build robustness.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **GC ephemeron correctness**: [#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732) and [#14722](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14722) both landed as part of a sustained effort by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to make ephemeron handling in `major_gc.c` correct before the new GC pacing logic ([#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)) lands.
- **Stdlib iteration API completeness**: [#14792](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14792) proposes `Weak.foldi_left` as a zero-allocation primitive, consistent with the broader pattern of adding fold/iter variants to stdlib collection modules.
- **Cross-compilation support**: Both [#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808) (minor build fixes) and [#14802](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14802) (configure argument default detection) reflect ongoing work to make OCaml reliable when cross-compiling or installing on less common Linux distributions.
- **Pretty-printer accuracy**: Both [#14797](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14797) (package type attributes) and issue [#14809](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14809) (Ptyp_functor extensions) highlight continued attention to round-trip fidelity in `pprintast`, important for tools like Merlin and ppx rewriters.
repositories.contributors_summary: Gabriel Scherer, David Allsopp, Antonin Décimo, and chetmurthy were the most active contributors, with Florian Angeletti, Jacques Garrigue, and Stefan Muenzel driving open PR work.

repositories.brief_summary: API cleanup with chdir removal, new path name modules, and new issues around Windows plugin DLL loading and lock file disjunction.
repositories.new_features_summary: New OpamPathName and OpamRepositoryPathName modules; OpamSystem commands gain ?dir argument replacing chdir-based APIs.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`OpamPathName`** module added to `opam-format` to specify names of internal opam root elements (files and directories), eliminating hardcoded strings across the codebase ([#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou))
- New **`OpamRepositoryPathName`** module added to specify names of repository layout elements, alongside moving `OpamRepositoryPath` from `opam-repository` to `opam-format` for better consistency ([#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou))
- **`OpamSystem.{command,commands,read_command_output}`** gained an optional `?dir: dirname` argument to launch commands in a specific directory, replacing the pattern of calling `chdir` before spawning processes ([#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb))
- **`OpamFilter.expand_interpolations_in_file`** argument type changed from `basename` to `filename`, enabling support for absolute paths in `opam config subst` (relates to [#6925](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6925)) ([#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb))
- **Removed chdir-based APIs**: `OpamFilename.in_dir`, `OpamSystem.in_tmp_dir`, `OpamSystem.in_dir`, and `OpamSystem.chdir` removed from the public API ([#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb))
- **Manual documentation** updated to replace the OCaml REPL snippet for version comparison with a direct link to the [OpamVersionCompare](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/pull/6809) API, and adds a `lib-tests` target to the main Makefile for simpler test invocation ([#6809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6809), [#6928](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6928) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate))
- **`man2html`** in the doc build now processes only `.1` man page files instead of all files in the man directory, fixing spurious empty files and error messages in the generated docs ([#6807](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6807) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate))
repositories.activity_summary: Three new PRs opened for Solaris terminal resizing, shell exit code leaks, and patch module export; six PRs merged covering API and CI improvements.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened**: [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened [#6934](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6934) to export patch-related functions into a new `OpamPatch` module, marked ready to merge and intended to simplify the rebase of the larger [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625) work
- **Newly opened**: [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) opened [#6933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6933) to add **terminal resizing detection on Solaris/Illumos**, correcting the SIGWINCH signal number (28 on Linux/macOS/BSDs, 20 on Illumos), requires OCaml 5.4 or newer
- **Newly opened**: [Talel Zighni](https://github.com/ta2005) opened [#6930](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6930) to fix the **opam-init shell scripts** for Zsh and Fish leaking a non-zero exit code when the opam environment is already initialized, affecting users with status-code-displaying prompts
- **Ongoing (draft)**: [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) has an open draft [#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923) that adds a **prompt to remove auto-pinned packages** when `opam install` fails or is aborted, queued behind [#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)
- **Ongoing**: [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) continues work on [#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680) to **abstract over repository roots**, enabling simpler addition of new repository formats; marked ready to merge but queued on [#6679](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6679)
- **Ongoing**: [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib) has an open PR [#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921) to **homogenise manpage reference highlighting**, standardizing command options and environment variables to bold `$(b, ...)` formatting
- **Merged**: CI workflow updated to trigger on changes in `doc/` ([#6927](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6927)), and `lib-tests` Makefile target added ([#6928](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6928)), closing the long-standing issue [#6810](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6810) by [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- **New issues opened**: [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) filed two Windows portability issues — [#6931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6931) covering opam plugins that require DLLs (e.g. `opam-publish` with libgmp) failing on startup in PowerShell due to DLL path resolution, and [#6932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6932) tracking a broader design question of how to allow dynlinked executables to run in native Windows shells
- **New issue**: [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) filed [#6935](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6935) reporting that lock files do not use **disjunction for transitive dependencies** of `with-doc` and `with-dev-setup`, where shared transitive deps (e.g. `cmdliner`) only get annotated with one flag instead of `(with-doc | with-dev-setup)`
repositories.discussions_summary: Active discussion on .install file prefix field design and ongoing debate on env_hook path clobbering behavior.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- Discussion on [#6919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6919) (add a `prefix` field to `.install` files) clarified the intended semantics: the field should specify a subdirectory under the switch prefix (not the prefix itself), enabling cross-compiled packages (e.g. Windows sysroot) to install files under the switch prefix. [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) has a working prototype and [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) confirmed the approach looks correct
- The long-standing [#6815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6815) on `env_hook` aggressively overriding PATH continues to attract attention, with [Illia Denysenko](https://github.com/juliancoffee) noting a similar experience when trying to use a custom-built `dune`; the issue proposes a direnv-style approach of only updating PATH if the switch directory is not already present
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) commented on [#3709](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/3709) (Ctrl-C reliability during installation) that the issue likely got fixed between opam 2.1 and 2.2 via [c4b2e0f](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/commit/c4b2e0fd844eabba0bf635f0a4c8d3cb22c0005d), with [shym](https://github.com/shym) unable to reproduce on opam 2.5.1
repositories.trends_summary: Continued focus on API cleanliness (chdir removal, path name modules) and Windows/cross-platform portability improvements.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The **chdir removal** effort ([#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)) continues a pattern of making the opam library safer for use in multithreaded contexts by replacing global working-directory mutations with explicit `?dir` arguments passed to system commands
- **Windows and cross-platform portability** is receiving sustained attention, with new issues filed around DLL loading for plugins ([#6931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6931), [#6932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6932)), terminal signal handling on Solaris ([#6933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6933)), and ongoing work on `.install` file prefix fields for cross-compilation ([#6919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6919))
- **Repository abstraction** is an ongoing theme with both `OpamRepositoryPath` being moved to `opam-format` ([#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917)) and the larger repository root abstraction PR ([#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680)) nearing readiness, laying groundwork for new repository formats in [#6625](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6625)
repositories.contributors_summary: Kate, Nathan Rebours, and R. Boujbel drove most merged work; Talel Zighni and Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA contributed community PRs.
repositories.contributors:
- [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate) merged documentation improvements ([#6809](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6809), [#6807](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6807)), CI improvements ([#6927](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6927), [#6928](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6928)), opened new PRs for patch module export ([#6934](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6934)) and Solaris terminal resize support ([#6933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6933)), filed two Windows portability issues ([#6931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6931), [#6932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6932)), and provided review feedback on multiple community PRs
- [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) had [#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910) merged (chdir removal and `?dir` argument addition), and has an ongoing draft PR [#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923) for cleaning up auto-pinned packages on install failure
- [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) had [#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917) merged adding `OpamPathName` and `OpamRepositoryPathName` modules, and provided review suggestions on [#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910) and [#6680](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6680)
- [Talel Zighni](https://github.com/ta2005) opened [#6930](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6930) fixing shell init script exit code leaks in Zsh and Fish, and updated the PR in response to review feedback from [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)
- [Guilherme FACCINI BANDEIRA](https://github.com/faccinib) has an open PR [#6921](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6921) to homogenise manpage highlighting for options and environment variables
- [Brian Ward](https://github.com/WardBrian) actively participated in the [#6919](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6919) design discussion, presenting a working prototype for the `.install` file `prefix` field and iterating on the design based on [Kate](https://github.com/kit-ty-kate)'s feedback
- [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) filed [#6935](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6935) reporting the lock file disjunction issue for `with-doc`/`with-dev-setup` transitive dependencies, and identified the likely relevant code in `opamLockCommand.ml`
- [Illia Denysenko](https://github.com/juliancoffee) commented on [#6815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6815) with a confirming experience of `env_hook` overriding custom tool paths

repositories.brief_summary: FreeBSD 15.0 build failure investigated and resolved via workaround; Nix flake dependencies updated.
repositories.activity_summary: Automated Nix flake lock update opened; FreeBSD 15.0 build failure issue investigated and closed with a workaround patch.
repositories.activity:
- The automated **flake.lock dependency update** PR [#1437](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1437) by `github-actions` remains open, updating `merlin5_3` (to 2026-05-04 snapshot) and `nixpkgs` (to 2026-05-16 snapshot) inputs.
- **FreeBSD 15.0 build failure** [#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605) was reported and self-investigated by [Zaraksh Rahman](https://github.com/zarakshR): the vendored `libev` in the `lev` submodule unconditionally includes `<sys/statfs.h>`, which is Linux-specific and absent on FreeBSD 15.0+. A manual patch applying a conditional `<sys/mount.h>` include for FreeBSD resolves the build; an upstream fix was submitted to [rgrinberg/lev#101](https://github.com/rgrinberg/lev/pull/101).
repositories.discussions_summary: FreeBSD build failure prompted community self-help discussion with a working patch shared in the issue thread.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Zaraksh Rahman](https://github.com/zarakshR) documented the **FreeBSD 15.0 build failure** root cause in [#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605), linking it to prior issues [#1459](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1459), [#747](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/747), and [#1069](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1069), and shared a **`git am` patch** for others to apply manually while awaiting an upstream `lev` fix.
repositories.trends_summary: FreeBSD compatibility with the vendored lev/libev dependency remains a recurring build problem.
repositories.emerging_trends: - **FreeBSD compatibility** with the vendored `lev` submodule's `libev` has triggered repeated build failures across multiple releases ([#1459](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1459), [#747](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/747), [#1069](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1069), [#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605)), suggesting the project may benefit from tracking the upstream `lev` fix at [rgrinberg/lev#101](https://github.com/rgrinberg/lev/pull/101) or applying the BSD-specific header guard directly in the submodule.
repositories.contributors_summary: Zaraksh Rahman investigated and documented the FreeBSD build failure with a working patch.
repositories.contributors: - [Zaraksh Rahman](https://github.com/zarakshR) opened [#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605), diagnosed the `<sys/statfs.h>` missing header root cause in the vendored `libev`, developed a patch, and shared step-by-step workaround instructions for other affected users.

repositories.brief_summary: Two bug-fix PRs and an ambitious structural search feature proposal opened, with no merges to main this week.
repositories.activity_summary: Three new PRs opened targeting locate correctness, signature-help accuracy, and a structural search subcommand.
repositories.activity:
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) opened [#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066) fixing **locate in let punnings**, where punned bindings share a location causing `locate` to land on the pattern side and return "Already at definition point" instead of navigating to the definition — resolves [#1796](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1796)
- [Carine Morel](https://github.com/lyrm) opened [#2067](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2067) fixing **signature-help with type aliases**: the query now expands type aliases one level to determine whether a value is a function, enabling signature help for cases like `type t = int -> unit; let f : t = ...` — resolves [#1927](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/1927)
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) updated [#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064), a proposal to integrate **ocamlgrep structural search** into Merlin as a new `ocamlgrep` subcommand, using `dune describe workspace` to discover `.cmt` files and an `Expr_search` module to match expressions by structure and type across the project
- Dependabot opened [#2065](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2065) bumping **cachix/install-nix-action** from 31.8.4 to 31.10.6 (includes Nix security fixes); an older version of the same bump ([#2061](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2061)) was closed without merging on 2026-05-11
repositories.discussions_summary: Xavier Van de Woestyne commented positively on the locate fix PR.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) responded with "Neat!" to the **locate fix for let punnings** in [#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066), indicating early approval from a Merlin maintainer
repositories.trends_summary: Contributors are focusing on correctness of editor-facing queries (locate, signature-help) and exploring project-wide analysis tools.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- Two independent PRs ([#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066), [#2067](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2067)) target **query correctness** for editor interactions — locate and signature-help — suggesting ongoing efforts to harden Merlin's responses at edge cases in the OCaml syntax
- The **ocamlgrep integration** proposal ([#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064)) points toward Merlin evolving beyond single-file analysis into project-wide structural code search, reusing `.cmt` file infrastructure already present in the toolchain
repositories.contributors_summary: Three contributors opened PRs this week; one Merlin maintainer provided review feedback.
repositories.contributors:
- [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon) continued work on the ocamlgrep integration ([#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064)), which received an update on 2026-05-13
- [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) opened the locate fix for let punnings ([#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)) with a detailed explanation of the underlying location invariant issue
- [Carine Morel](https://github.com/lyrm) opened the signature-help type alias fix ([#2067](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2067)) with test cases documenting both the fixed and still-open edge cases
- [Xavier Van de Woestyne](https://github.com/xvw) reviewed and commented on [#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)

repositories.brief_summary: Dune bumped its language version to 3.24, removed the deprecated (lang coq) build language, added allocation profiling, and made significant RPC socket lifecycle fixes.
repositories.new_features_summary: New features include allocation profiling via DUNE_TRACE=+alloc, conditional melange.modules field, RPC client count in the watch status line, and a registry write event.
repositories.new_features:
- **Allocation profiling** added via `DUNE_TRACE=+alloc`; results appear in the trace and can be queried with `dune trace cat | jq 'select(.cat == "alloc")'` ([#14401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14401), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **Conditional `(melange.modules ..)` field** added to the Melange extension, gated on Dune language 3.24 ([#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14482), [#14573](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14573), [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro))
- **RPC client count** is now shown in the watch mode status line as `[rpc N]` when clients are connected ([#14489](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14489), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **Registry write event** emitted to trace when the running RPC is registered to XDG ([#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14536), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **Dune language version bumped to 3.24** to open the development cycle for the next release ([#14559](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14559), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- **Directory targets promoted to stable** in Dune 3.24; they were experimental for a long period with no changes and many users already rely on them ([#14579](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14579), [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
repositories.activity_summary: Over 45 PRs were merged covering RPC socket lifecycle hardening, performance improvements, platform fixes, and a major cleanup removing the deprecated Coq build language.
repositories.activity:
- [#14574](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14574) **Delete Melange 0.1 in Dune 3.24** — draft PR to remove version 0.1 of the Melange extension, leaving only `(using melange 1.0)` supported ([Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro))
- [#14579](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14579) **Make directory targets available in Dune 3.24** — open PR to graduate directory targets from experimental ([Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- [#14550](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14550) **Scheduler: use semaphore for event wakeups** — open PR replacing the dedicated signal watcher thread with C signal handlers integrated into the main event loop ([Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- [#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14544) **Add oxcaml-compiler to compiler packages** — open PR to recognise the renamed OxCaml compiler package so Dune treats it as a toolchain ([Sudha Parimala](https://github.com/Sudha247))
- [#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14525) **Removed deprecated `(lang coq)` Coq Build Language** — projects declaring `(using coq <version>)` now get an error pointing to Rocq, closing [#12788](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/12788) ([Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))
- [#14401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14401) **Allocation profiling feature merged** — adds a lightweight allocation tracker controllable via `DUNE_TRACE=+alloc` ([Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- [#14568](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14568) **RPC: clean up partially-bound server sockets** — fixes fd leaks and stranded Unix socket paths when multi-address server creation fails partway ([Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- [#14565](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14565) **RPC: unlink unix sockets when stopping unserved server** — fixes socket paths left in the filesystem if a server is stopped before being served ([Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- [#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14580) **RPC: return bound addresses for listening server sockets** — TCP listeners with port 0 now report the kernel-assigned port via `getsockname` ([Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- [#14556](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14556) **Artifact substitution: compare executable bits when replacing files** — repairs executable bit if not set correctly in the workspace ([Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- [#14524](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14524) **Fix lock subdirectories** — `lock_dir` paths containing subdirectories (e.g. `sub/dune.lock`) no longer fail with "No rule found", closing [#14523](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14523) ([Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))
- [#14460](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14460) **Faster file digest** — combines open/digest/close and stat into a single thread operation ([Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg))
- [#14543](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14543) **Fix Option.hash collision** — `Option.hash f None` and `Option.hash f (Some x)` previously collided when `f x = 0` ([Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- [#14513](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14513) **Infra: Lib_index and raw ocamldep** — Layer 1 of a 9-layer stack implementing per-module library dependency filtering ([Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb))
- [#14278](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14278) **Path: appending local paths to external paths** — uses `/` as directory separator on all platforms for constructed paths; on Windows normalises process paths to `\` before spawning ([Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))
- [#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14512) **Fix NetBSD bootstrap** — includes `<sys/wait.h>` in `lev_stubs.c`, matching the FreeBSD/OpenBSD guard, closing [#14484](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14484) ([Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))
- [#14529](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14529) **Capture right dune in subshell** — test fix ensuring subshells pick up the in-development dune binary ([Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV))
- [#14527](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14527) **Update odoc constraint for new markdown rendering** — bumps to `odoc >= 3.2.0` to fix CI failures caused by changed rendering in odoc 3.2.0 ([Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV))
repositories.discussions_summary: Key discussions covered the OCaml minimum version lower bound debate, ergonomics improvements for `dune exec`, and the complexity of merging menhir version constraints in generated opam files.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **OCaml minimum version lower bound** ([#14506](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14506)) — ongoing debate about reverting the 4.14 bump (introduced in 3.23.0) back to 4.11, with [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) arguing against the revert and [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) proposing to collect concrete user impact reports before deciding
- **`dune exec` ergonomics** ([#14510](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14510), [#14511](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14511)) — [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) proposed allowing `dune exec` to infer the target when unambiguous; [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) proposed a directory-based disambiguation approach
- **Menhir constraint merging** ([#14453](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14453)) — extended discussion about whether `dune` should recursively descend into opam constraint expressions to deduplicate lower bounds; concluded that the added complexity is not worth the benefit at this stage
- **OxCaml compiler package recognition** ([#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14544)) — [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) raised whether `flags: compiler` in opam metadata should be used instead of hardcoding package names; [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) explained the current "toolchain hack" for non-relocatable compilers and the roadmap to remove it once OxCaml becomes relocatable
- **`dune-configurator` OCaml version constraint** ([#14548](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14548)) — [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) reported that `String.ends_with` (added without a compat shim) causes build failures on OCaml < 4.13; [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) suggested raising the lower bound to 4.14
repositories.trends_summary: Development focus is shifting toward RPC infrastructure hardening, incremental build performance improvements, and preparatory refactoring for Dune 3.24 feature graduation.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Extensive RPC socket lifecycle hardening** — multiple PRs ([#14565](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14565), [#14568](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14568), [#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14580)) fix fd leaks, stranded socket paths, and incorrect address reporting, indicating a push toward production-grade RPC reliability
- **Per-module library dependency filtering** — [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) is developing a 9-layer stack ([#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14492)) to narrow compile-rule dependencies to only the libraries each module actually uses, promising improved incremental build performance
- **Performance-focused allocation reduction** — multiple contributions ([#14460](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14460), [#14533](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14533), [#14534](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14534), [#14543](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14543)) targeting lower memory allocations in hot paths, supported by the new allocation profiling feature ([#14401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14401))
- **Signal handling rework** — PR [#14550](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14550) replaces the signal watching thread (which `sigwait` makes unreliable on macOS) with C signal handlers integrated directly into the scheduler event loop
- **Melange integration maturing** — conditional modules ([#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14482)) and cleanup of the 0.1 extension version ([#14574](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14574)) signal stabilisation of the Melange build language toward 3.24
repositories.issues_summary: One new good-first-issue was opened; several platform compatibility and package management bugs were closed during the 3.23.1 release cycle.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [#14560](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14560) **Error message when dune lang version is too new in dune-package is unclear** — when a user downgrades dune and encounters a version mismatch in an installed `.dune-package` file, the current error points at the version number rather than suggesting to upgrade dune ([Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter))
repositories.contributors_summary: Active contributors this week were led by Rudi Grinberg with a large volume of RPC work, alongside Ali Caglayan, Antonio Nuno Monteiro, Robin Bate Boerop, and Marek Kubica.

repositories.brief_summary: Discussion on persistent compiler processes RFC continued with a suggestion to expose a cleaner compiler API.
repositories.activity_summary: Ongoing RFC [#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60) on persistent compiler processes received a review comment.
repositories.activity: - The **persistent compiler processes** RFC ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60)) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb), open since January 2026, received a comment from [@copy](https://github.com/copy) suggesting that exposing a more ergonomic compiler API (removing global state from compiler modules) could be a complementary or alternative approach, enabling build tools like dune and Merlin to experiment with different protocols and supporting finer-grained parallelisation inside the compiler.
repositories.discussions_summary: RFC #60 discussion surfaced an alternative design direction: exposing a stable compiler API rather than a persistent-process protocol.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [@copy](https://github.com/copy) commented on the **persistent compiler processes** RFC ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60)) proposing that a more ergonomic (though not necessarily stable) compiler API—achieved by removing global state from compiler modules—could achieve similar build-time benefits while also benefiting Merlin and enabling finer-grained parallelisation, rather than requiring a dedicated persistent-process protocol.
repositories.trends_summary: Interest in reducing per-invocation compilation overhead, whether via persistent processes or a cleaner compiler API.
repositories.emerging_trends: - Continued interest in **reducing compilation overhead** for tools like dune, with debate in [#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60) touching on whether a persistent-process model or a cleaner compiler API is the better approach to eliminating redundant `.cmi` unmarshalling and process-startup costs.
repositories.contributors_summary: [@copy](https://github.com/copy) provided design feedback on RFC #60; [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) is the PR author.
repositories.contributors:
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) authored the **persistent compiler processes** RFC ([#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60)), which proposes a server mode for `ocamlc`, `ocamlopt`, `ocamldep`, and related tools to reduce repeated startup and `.cmi` unmarshalling costs across build invocations.
- [@copy](https://github.com/copy) participated in the design discussion on [#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60), offering an alternative perspective centred on removing global state from compiler modules as a path toward both build-time improvements and better tooling integration.

repositories.brief_summary: Release 3.2.1 shipped with bug fixes for xref2 substitution crashes; OxCaml unboxed type support merged.
repositories.new_features_summary: OxCaml unboxed named types now rendered correctly; voodoo include-path resolution fixed for transitive deps.
repositories.new_features:
- **OxCaml unboxed named types** are now supported in generated documentation — the trailing `#` suffix is correctly propagated from the compiler's `Pextra_ty (_, Punboxed_ty)` representation ([#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w)
- **Voodoo driver include-path fix** — transitive closure of `lib_deps` is now computed before emitting per-compile `-I` flags, resolving missing include paths when building docs in voodoo mode ([#1434](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1434)) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
- **Substitution assertion fix for `merlin-lib`** — a long-standing assertion failure in `resolved_signature_fragment` when handling substituted module types is now caught and handled gracefully, fixing documentation builds for packages like `merlin-lib` ([#1430](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1430)) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
- **Local module substitution exception fix** — unrecoverable exception in `apply_inner_substs` when a local module substitution cannot be resolved is now detected and the signature is left in its original form, restoring compatibility with `core_unix` ([#1433](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1433)) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
repositories.activity_summary: Two OxCaml PRs remain open; odoc 3.2.1 released with three crash fixes; opam conflict constraint and bench deps updated.
repositories.activity:
- **Open: OxCaml zero alloc annotation** PR by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) adds support for `[@@zero_alloc]` on signatures, reading the annotation from compiler artefacts (`val_zero_alloc`) rather than re-parsing attributes, following guidance from [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422))
- **Open: OxCaml modality support** PR by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) builds on [#1410](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1410) to render modalities in documentation, addressing issue [#1416](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1416); review ongoing with [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420))
- **Release 3.2.1** published on 2026-05-12 by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam), bundling the fixes for [#1426](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1426) (`base` breakage in OCaml 5.5.0), [#1429](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1429) (`merlin-lib` assertion failure), and [#1431](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1431) (regression from 3.2.0 in local module substitution)
- **OxCaml opam conflict constraint** added by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) to prevent installation with OxCaml versions older than 5.2.0minus31 ([#1432](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1432))
- **odoc-bench package versions updated** to keep the benchmark suite working with current dependencies ([#1435](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1435)) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
- **CHANGES.md syntax fix** correcting malformed whitespace that broke rendered changelog display, by [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) ([#1436](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1436))
repositories.discussions_summary: Review discussion on #1422 clarified that zero_alloc info is available directly in compiler artefacts.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- On the **zero alloc annotation PR** [#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) identified that OxCaml already stores `val_zero_alloc` in cmi/cmti artefacts, prompting [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) to rewrite the implementation to read from compiler data rather than reparsing attributes — removing significant parsing code
- On **Fix 1431** [#1433](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1433), [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) clarified the layered regression history: [#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1400) fixed [#930](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/930) but introduced [#1431](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1431); the current PR restores pre-[#1400](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1400) behaviour only in the failing case, leaving a narrower residual edge case
repositories.trends_summary: OxCaml-specific feature work continues in parallel with stabilization patches targeting real-world package failures.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml feature parity** is an active track this week, with unboxed named types merged ([#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)), modalities in review ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)), and zero alloc annotations in progress ([#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)), all targeting correct rendering of OxCaml-specific type and value annotations
- **Stabilization driven by real-world packages** — the 3.2.1 release was triggered by failures in well-known packages (`base`, `merlin-lib`, `core_unix`), and fixes were turned around and released within days of reports ([#1429](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1429), [#1431](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1431))
- **xref2 substitution logic** continues to be a source of edge-case bugs; two separate crashes this week traced back to `apply_inner_substs` and `resolved_signature_fragment` in `src/xref2/`, both connected to the earlier [#930](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/930) fix chain
repositories.issues_summary: One good-first-issue open: polymorphic variant inherit comments not rendered, recently confirmed reproducible.
repositories.good_first_issues: - [**Comments attached to polymorphic variant inherit are not rendered**](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/846) — doc comments on inherited polymorphic variant rows (e.g. `| bar (** bar *)`) are silently dropped in generated output; recently confirmed reproducible by [Charlène Gros](https://github.com/Lucccyo), making this a good starting point for contributors interested in the odoc model layer ([#846](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/846))
repositories.contributors_summary: Jon Ludlam drove the 3.2.1 release; art-w merged OxCaml unboxed types; Marek Kubica and David Allsopp contributed fixes and new work.
repositories.contributors:
- [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) authored fixes for the `merlin-lib` assertion crash ([#1430](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1430)), the `core_unix` substitution exception ([#1433](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1433)), the voodoo include-path regression ([#1434](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1434)), a bench deps update ([#1435](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1435)), and coordinated the 3.2.1 release
- [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) merged the OxCaml unboxed named types feature ([#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)) and has the OxCaml modalities PR under active review ([#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420))

repositories.brief_summary: QuickJS-NG runtime support merged, major DOM API expansion, and browser compatibility documentation added.
repositories.new_features_summary: QuickJS-NG support, expanded DOM/Performance bindings, browser-compat Babel guide, and Wasm toplevel size optimization.
repositories.new_features:
- **QuickJS-NG runtime support** added via a new `quickjs` dune profile with a `quickjs_wrapper.exe` thin wrapper, a real-disk filesystem (`fs_quickjs.js`) backed by the `qjs` `os`/`std` modules (opt-in via `+fs_quickjs.js`), byte-faithful stdout/stderr over `os.write`, a `Sys.getenv` fallback via `std.getenviron()`, and a CI job running the full test suite against QuickJS-NG ([#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229))
- **`caml_sys_system_command`** implemented via `os.exec` under QuickJS, enabling `Sys.command` in the QuickJS profile ([f9e5678](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/f9e56785a6e9d008bc9b0d6448e6a457635a615d))
- **DOM API expansion** in `Dom` and `Dom_html`: new `HTMLCollection` class type (`collection`) with `namedItem`; `node` extended with `parentElement`, `isConnected`, `contains`, `getRootNode`, `isEqualNode`, `isSameNode`, `normalize`; `element` gains `localName`, `prefix`, `hasAttributes`, `toggleAttribute`, `getAttributeNames`; `getElementsByTagName` and `getElementsByClassName` now return the more specific `collection` type ([#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221))
- **`Performance` module** added to `lib/js_of_ocaml`, binding `window.performance` including `performance.now()` (closes long-standing request [#679](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/679)) ([#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221))
- **`selection*` attributes on input elements** now bound, closing [#453](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/453) ([#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221))
- **Type corrections** in `Dom_html`: `inputElement.defaultChecked` corrected from `js_string t prop` to `bool t prop`; `createVideo` return type corrected from `audioElement t` to `videoElement t`; `selection.collapse` corrected to match the actual JS signature `(node opt -> unit meth)` ([#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221))
- **Wasm: constant expressions globalized** — values placed into Wasm globals reduce the size of the toplevel function by ~50% for the PRT benchmark ([#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211))
- **WHATWG text codec fallback tightened** — the pure-JS UTF-8 fallback (used when `TextDecoder` is unavailable) now validates continuation bytes per the WHATWG default error-handling rule instead of greedily consuming bytes on any high-bit lead ([c87176e](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/commit/c87176e7e39b763400abc0390573fb585ba702c0))
- **`Intl` load-time fix**: `Intl.{Collator,DateTimeFormat,...}` member lookups are now deferred so the `Intl` module no longer throws at load time on hosts where `globalThis.Intl` is undefined (e.g. QuickJS) ([#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229))
- **Browser compatibility manual chapter** (`manual/browser-compat.wiki`) added, documenting the Babel + core-js recipe for targeting browsers older than ES2020, bundling with esbuild, and integrating into dune builds ([#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216))
- **Browser bundling recipe updated** from `--target-env=browser` to `--external:node:*` in esbuild, allowing the same compiled `.js` to work for both Node and browser bundling ([#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216))
repositories.activity_summary: Five PRs merged covering QuickJS support, DOM expansion, Wasm optimization, and docs; four PRs remain open including Wasm loop extraction and Promise/Fetch bindings.
repositories.activity:
- **Newly opened this week**: [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) opened the **Wasm runtime small fixes** PR ([#2240](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2240)) and [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) opened **Promise + Fetch bindings** PR ([#2239](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2239)), which closes long-standing requests [#2031](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2031) and [#596](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/596) with a full `Promise`, `Abort`, and `Fetch` module suite
- **Ongoing open PRs**: [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)'s **Wasm loop extraction** draft ([#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212)) moves loops out of the large toplevel function to improve Wasm engine tier-up efficiency; the **dune 3.23 migration** draft ([#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)) is blocked on upstream dune PRs [ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441) and [ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438); [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)'s **wasm-opt removal** draft ([#2238](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2238)) replaces `--opt 1` with targeted local variable optimizations, cutting wasm compilation time by ~60%; update to **OxCaml 5.2.0minus31** ([#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225)) is open
- **Merged this week**: [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) merged **QuickJS-NG support** ([#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)), **browser-compat documentation** ([#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216)), and **ocamlformat bump** ([#2237](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2237)); [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) merged **DOM updates** ([#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221)) and **Wasm global variable placement** ([#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211))
- **Issues closed**: Five long-standing issues resolved this week — JS-agnostic IR request ([#1075](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1075)) closed as superseded by the wasm backend and current IR approach; `--target-env=neutral` request ([#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226)) closed as `isomorphic` already covers the use case; `window.performance.now` binding request ([#679](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/679)) and `selection*` on input elements ([#453](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/453)) both closed by [#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221); browser feature-detection request ([#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215)) closed after [#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216) merged
- **Ongoing open PRs from earlier weeks**: CSS Font Loading API wrapper by [Mesmera (WAFT)](https://github.com/mesmerizingwaft) ([#1833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1833)) noted as unblocked by [#2239](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2239); `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` bindings by [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) ([#2070](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2070)) and `onbeforeunload` handler fix by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) ([#2159](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2159)) remain open
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on QuickJS-NG PR about runtime-specific code placement and Debian packaging considerations.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) commented on the **QuickJS-NG support** PR ([#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)) noting a preference to keep runtime-specific code outside of jsoo itself (suggesting application developers or separate libraries should handle it), and flagged that quickjs (not quickjs-ng) is currently the only JS runtime available on all Debian architectures due to quickjs-ng FTBFS on Hurd
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) closed the **JS-agnostic IR** feature request ([#1075](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1075)) with a detailed explanation that the current IR is already JS-agnostic, wasm_of_ocaml demonstrates how to use jsoo as a library for new backends, and a tree-like IR is no longer the desired direction
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) closed the **neutral target-env** request ([#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226)) explaining that `isomorphic` mode (runtime feature detection) serves the same purpose, and the QuickJS-related issues were addressed by [#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229) and [#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216)
repositories.trends_summary: Expanding runtime target support beyond Node.js (QuickJS-NG) and growing the DOM/browser API surface to match modern web specifications.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Broadening JS runtime support beyond Node.js**: the QuickJS-NG integration ([#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)) represents a deliberate push to make js_of_ocaml usable in non-Node environments (embedded runtimes, Debian toolchains, etc.), following earlier `--target-env` work and the neutral-target discussions
- **Systematic DOM API catch-up**: [#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221) added hundreds of bindings in a single PR; the in-progress Promise/Fetch bindings ([#2239](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2239)) and CSS Font Loading ([#1833](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1833)) continue this pattern of aligning coverage with modern browser APIs
- **Wasm backend performance investment**: both [#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211) and the draft [#2238](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2238) target Wasm-specific optimizations (reducing toplevel function size, eliminating wasm-opt overhead), suggesting ongoing focus on making wasm_of_ocaml output faster to compile and execute
repositories.contributors_summary: [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) and [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) drove all merged work this week across runtime, library, and Wasm areas.
repositories.contributors:
- [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) merged the QuickJS-NG support PR ([#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)) which incorporated dozens of runtime commits across QuickJS filesystem, stdout/stderr handling, and CI integration; merged the browser compatibility documentation ([#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216)) and ocamlformat bump ([#2237](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2237)); opened Promise + Fetch bindings ([#2239](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2239)); and closed five issues with explanatory comments ([#1075](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1075), [#2226](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2226), [#2215](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2215), [#453](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/453), [#679](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/679))
- [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) merged the large DOM updates PR ([#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221)) and the Wasm global variable placement PR ([#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211)); opened Wasm runtime small fixes ([#2240](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2240)); and has ongoing draft PRs for Wasm loop extraction ([#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212)), wasm-opt removal ([#2238](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2238)), and OxCaml update ([#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225))
- [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) provided feedback on the QuickJS-NG PR ([#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)) with practical deployment context from the Debian packaging perspective

repositories.brief_summary: Active week with 34 merges across compiler backend optimisation, metaprogramming fixes, DWARF/debugging improvements, and major documentation updates for unboxed types and small integers.
repositories.new_features_summary: New features include preemption support, ikinds enablement, magic staged modes for metaprogramming, and expanded `-X` extra compiler parameter registration.
repositories.new_features:
- **Initial preemption implementation** merged by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) ([#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/4881)), adding the foundational preemption mechanism to the runtime.
- **Enable ikinds** merged by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) ([#5999](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5999)), turning on integer kind support in the compiler.
- **Add `[@magic_staged_modes]`** to delay mode checks until program generation, by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) ([#6066](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6066)), enabling quoting of non-legacy (e.g. local) expressions in metaprogramming.
- **Facility to register `-X` extra params** added by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#6003](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6003)), allowing internal undocumented compiler options to be registered via a new `Extra_options` module.
- **Use new LLDB OxCaml language plugin** integrated by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) ([#4961](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/4961)), switching to a dedicated OxCaml language plugin for LLDB.
- **Bump to OxCaml LLDB 21.1.0+oxcaml0** by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) ([#5996](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5996)), picking up name-mangling-related changes for the LLDB plugin.
- **Allow setting `-gdwarf-inlined-frames` via `OCAMLPARAM`** by [Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski) ([#6061](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6061)), enabling opam switches with extra debug info across all libraries.
- **Allow aliased interface files to not exist when building the cmi_bundle for metaprogramming** by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([#6043](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6043)), fixing crashes when module aliases had no CMI and preventing link-time dropping of quote-only modules.
- **Add user documentation for the `borrow_` operator** by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([#5928](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5928)), providing comprehensive documentation for the borrowing feature in the uniqueness extension.
- **Merlin: add support for injected parsetrees to mpipeline** by [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) ([#6078](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6078)), enabling faster-path completion by allowing Merlin to type-check a reduced parsetree.
repositories.activity_summary: 34 PRs were merged spanning compiler correctness fixes, backend optimisations, metaprogramming improvements, and tooling/documentation updates across 388 changed files.
repositories.activity:
- **Use total structural order for solver morph map keys** fixed by [julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([#6047](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6047)), correcting an unsound map comparison that could violate ordering contracts in the mode solver.
- **Fix compiler panic around `[@@unboxed]` and existentials** by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) ([#6046](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6046)), treating `Tof_kind` like other unknown types to avoid incorrect assumptions.
- **Fix scannable axis printing to meet** by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6060](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6060)), correcting printing of `or_null` kinds after recent lattice changes.
- **Fix kind annotation check for existential types in patterns** by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#5935](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5935)), fixing kind-checking for existential type variables in match patterns.
- **Avoid lifting switch tables with local coercions** by [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) ([#6084](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6084)), preventing incorrect code generation when switch tables include local coercions.
- **Fix stage-tracking fatal error when reporting inclusion error** by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) ([#6070](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6070)), stopping a compiler crash from hiding type errors in metaprogramming stage tracking.
- **Fix constructor logic when generating lambda for quotations** by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) ([#5983](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5983)), correcting Translquote for extension constructors and exceptions inside quotations.
- **Fix infix operator logic inside quotations** by [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) ([#5982](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5982)), resolving incorrect lambda generation for infix operators in metaprogramming quotes.
- **Fix a missing-parens bug in pprintast** by [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) ([#6052](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6052)), correcting the pretty-printer's parenthesisation output.
- **Reaper: fix bugs in rewriting of sets of closures** by [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) ([#6005](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6005)), correcting code-age relation handling and precision loss during closure-set rewriting.
- **Reaper: try to reduce `if` cascades** by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([#5938](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5938)), improving Flambda2 reaper code generation.
- **Revert "Flambda2: remove imported_names"** by [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) ([#6085](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6085)), reverting a Flambda2 change that caused regressions.
- **Make fatal errors related to array primitives more verbose** by [jdimino-js](https://github.com/jdimino-js) ([#6079](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6079)), adding location and pretty-printed arguments to array primitive fatal errors for easier debugging.
- **Add a warning for `[@untagged]` external int8/int16 returns** by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#6081](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6081)), alerting users to potentially incorrect sign-extension behaviour for small int returns.
- **Various unboxed types docs fixes** by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#6088](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6088)), correcting the unboxed array representation table and adding mixed-block version history.
- **Add more details about small int representation to docs** by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#6069](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6069)), documenting overflow behaviour and runtime representation of int8/int16.
- **Add a C interop section to small number docs** by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#6063](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6063)), adding documentation for using bits8/bits16 types in C FFI.
- **Fix mixed block v6 changelog** by [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) ([#6096](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6096)), correcting the changelog entry for mixed block representation v6.
- **Expect asm: Normalize labels** by [ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) ([#5993](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5993)), improving expect-asm test stability by normalising label names.
- **Binary emitter test for small integers** by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) ([#5942](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5942)), adding binary-level emit tests for small integer encodings.
- **5.4 testsuite and conflict resolutions** from [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) across multiple PRs ([#5972](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5972), [#5988](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5988), [#5989](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5989), [#6015](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6015)), resolving upstream OCaml 5.4 merge conflicts and updating test baselines.
repositories.discussions_summary: Key open PR discussions focused on the design of custom or_null payload kinds, DWARF emission standardisation, and the proper tracking of quoted module dependencies.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- **Custom `[@@or_null]` payload shape design** debated at length on [#5830](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5830) between [julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js), exploring "best-kind design" vs. "ordinary-scheme design" for user-defined nullable types.
- **DWARF emission standardisation** discussed on [#6093](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6093) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon), with [julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) raising concerns about correct `DW_AT_byte_stride` and element counts for packed unboxed arrays.
- **`Shape.Uid.t` and `Global_module.Name.t` integration** on [#6071](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6071) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923), with [julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) questioning potential loss of `-for-pack` information from declaration UIDs.
- **Frametables in `.rodata` section** on [#6094](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6094) by [Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski), with [julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) providing a macOS fix for Apple's assembler not accepting `.section .rodata`.
- **Regalloc rematerialisation** introduced as a draft in [#6077](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6077) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc), reporting 5% reduction in stack slots and 2.4% reduction in spills across compiler files.
- **Adding a Claude-based code review skill** proposed by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) in [#6072](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6072), with [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) trialling and reporting results with suggestions for pagination fixes.
repositories.trends_summary: The week showed sustained investment in metaprogramming correctness, compiler performance (compilation speed), and making DWARF debug info more standard and self-describing.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Metaprogramming maturity**: Multiple fixes landed for quotation and evaluation semantics ([#5982](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5982), [#5983](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5983), [#6043](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6043), [#6066](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6066), [#6070](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6070)), alongside larger open PRs like [#6086](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6086) and [#6092](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6092) pushing new quoting capabilities.
- **Compilation speed focus**: Several PRs labelled "compilation speed" remain open ([#6065](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6065), [#6068](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6068)) alongside benchmarking-only PRs from [julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([#6053](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6053), [#6054](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6054), [#6055](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6055), [#6058](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6058)) measuring regalloc and prologue validation overheads.
- **DWARF and debugging investment**: Standardising DWARF emission onto exprloc attributes ([#6093](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6093)) and adding a new LLDB language plugin ([#4961](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/4961)) reflect a sustained push toward better native debugging support.
- **Mode polymorphism stack**: A large stack of draft PRs from [ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) ([#5478](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5478) through [#5488](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5488)) continues to build towards functional mode polymorphism with level-based generalisation.

repositories.brief_summary: Draft PR for merging OCaml 5.4 upstream changes received an update, currently blocked by merge conflicts.
repositories.activity_summary: One open draft PR targeting OCaml 5.4 integration was updated, with merge conflicts still unresolved.
repositories.activity: - **Draft PR for 5.4 upstream merge** ([#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) opened by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) on 2026-05-05 and updated on 2026-05-11, bringing in a large set of changes (424,854 additions, 74,169 deletions across 888 files) to merge the "minus-37" branch; currently marked as a draft and in a **CONFLICTING** mergeable state, indicating ongoing rebase or conflict resolution work before it can land.
