repositories.brief_summary: CI odoc pin workaround removed after upstream odoc release; request to bump Jane Street preview packages to v0.18~preview.130.100+614.
repositories.new_features_summary: Upstream odoc released; CI no longer pins odoc to a git commit workaround.
repositories.new_features: - Removed the **temporary odoc CI pin** that had been pinning `odoc`, `odoc-parser`, and `sherlodoc` to a specific upstream git commit (`9695cd79`) pending release of odoc >= 3.2.0; now using the released upstream versions directly ([#45](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/45), [d8856c0](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/commit/d8856c02806463b3235943c68f52d824d22756ea))
repositories.activity_summary: Open issue requests bump to Jane Street v0.18~preview.130.100+614; CI odoc workaround merged and removed.
repositories.activity:
- [Artem Pianykh](https://github.com/artempyanykh) opened [#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46) requesting a **package bump to `v0.18~preview.130.100+614`** for Jane Street packages, noting that ~30 packages (including `async_smtp`, `bonsai_term`, `hardcaml_*`, `ocaml_simd`, `oxcaml_intrinsics`, `torch`, `unboxed`, `vec`, and others) are missing a release commit for this version; the issue references the upstream fix for [janestreet/ocaml_intrinsics_kernel#5](https://github.com/janestreet/ocaml_intrinsics_kernel/issues/5) and a prior bump at commit `7dd5def` to `v0.18~preview.130.91+190`
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) merged [#45](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/45) **"Unleash upstream odoc in CI"**, reverting the temporary pin of `odoc`/`odoc-parser`/`sherlodoc` to a pre-release git commit that had been in place while awaiting the upstream odoc 3.2.0 release
repositories.discussions_summary: Community request for Jane Street preview package bump highlights dependency on upstream release cadence.
repositories.notable_discussions: - [Artem Pianykh](https://github.com/artempyanykh) raised [#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46) noting that a prior bulk bump (`7dd5def`) updated 311 packages to `v0.18~preview.130.91+190`, and that a follow-up to `130.100+614` is needed, with a detailed list of ~30 packages still missing the newer release commit; references the upstream `ocaml_intrinsics_kernel` issue ([janestreet/ocaml_intrinsics_kernel#5](https://github.com/janestreet/ocaml_intrinsics_kernel/issues/5)) as motivation
repositories.trends_summary: CI infrastructure is being kept current by removing temporary workarounds as upstream dependencies stabilise.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The repo follows a pattern of **temporary CI pins** to unblock development while upstream packages stabilise, then promptly removing them once releases land — as seen with the odoc 3.2.0 pin lifecycle in [#45](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/45)
- **Bulk Jane Street preview package bumps** appear to be a recurring maintenance activity (prior bump `7dd5def` covered 311 packages), with community members like [Artem Pianykh](https://github.com/artempyanykh) actively tracking which packages lag behind in [#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46)
repositories.contributors_summary: David Allsopp merged the odoc CI cleanup; Artem Pianykh filed a package bump request.
repositories.contributors:
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) opened and merged [#45](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/45), removing the temporary odoc git-pin workaround from the CI action configuration
- [Artem Pianykh](https://github.com/artempyanykh) filed [#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46) with a detailed analysis of which packages need updating in the next `v0.18~preview.130.100+614` bulk bump, including a partial PR draft (`600382b`) to investigate the gap

repositories.brief_summary: New Uring.Res module for type-safe result handling, CLOEXEC fix for recvmsg FDs, and ZFS write_fixed warning added.
repositories.new_features_summary: Uring.Res module added with int/fd conversion helpers and pretty-printer; MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC applied atomically when receiving FDs.
repositories.new_features:
- New **`Uring.Res` module** providing a `private int` type for uring operation results, replacing raw `int` returns in the `completion_option` type. The module includes `int_result`/`int_exn` for numeric results, `fd_result`/`fd_exn` for file descriptor results, and a `pp` pretty-printer that emits symbolic error names (e.g. `ENOENT`) rather than negative integers — resolving [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) without requiring `Obj.magic` in user code ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131))
- **`MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`** flag is now set atomically when receiving file descriptors via `recvmsg`, preventing FD leakage to child processes in multi-threaded programs ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132))
- **`write_fixed` documentation** now includes a warning about a hang bug affecting old ZFS versions, linking to the tracking issue ([#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133), closes [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113))
repositories.activity_summary: Four PRs merged covering the new Res API, a CLOEXEC bug fix, ZFS documentation, and a test hang fix; two long-standing draft PRs closed.
repositories.activity:
- **`Uring.Res` module** merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), drawing on earlier prototype work by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) in [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130); the adopted approach uses a `private int` to avoid extra C call overhead while still providing type-safe fd and error conversions ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131))
- **CLOEXEC bug fix** for `recvmsg`-based FD passing merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), addressing a race condition in multi-threaded programs where a child process could inherit FDs before the flag was set manually ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132))
- **ZFS warning documentation** added to `write_fixed` API, prompted by a suggestion from [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal), closing the long-standing ZFS spin-hang bug report ([#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133), closes [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113))
- **Test hang fix** on Linux 5.10 merged: when the sending side of a test fails, the test no longer waits for a receive that will never arrive ([#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134))
- **Two draft PRs closed** without merging: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) (fd-from-int via extra C call) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) (fully typed wait results encoding type in job ID), both superseded by the lighter-weight approach in [#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)
- **Three old issues closed**: WSL2 `sendmsg` failure [#55](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/55) closed as presumed resolved by WSL kernel updates; `Uring.accept` unusable FD [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) closed as fixed by [#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131); ZFS write hang [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113) closed with documentation warning in [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133)
repositories.discussions_summary: Design discussion on type-safe result handling compared three approaches before settling on the private-int Res module.
repositories.notable_discussions: - **Type-safe result API design** debated across three PRs: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) added an extra C call to convert ints to FDs safely; [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) explored encoding the expected return type in 4 bits of the job ID so C stubs could return typed OCaml blocks. [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) noted in [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) that the fully-typed approach introduced allocation overhead and complicated Eio integration (unknown error codes becoming `EUNKNOWNERR`, additional match arms per operation), ultimately favoring the `private int` approach in [#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131) as the right balance of safety and compatibility
repositories.trends_summary: API ergonomics and safety improvements are a recurring theme, with several old issues cleared through targeted, minimal-overhead fixes.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- **Reducing unsafe patterns in the public API**: the `Uring.Res` module ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)) eliminates the need for `Obj.magic` when converting uring result integers to `Unix.file_descr`, continuing a broader push to make the library's surface safe by default without imposing runtime overhead
- **Proactive OS-compatibility hardening**: the `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` fix ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132)) and the Linux 5.10 test hang fix ([#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134)) reflect attention to correctness across kernel versions and environments beyond the primary development platform
repositories.contributors_summary: Thomas Leonard drove all merged code this week; David Allsopp's earlier prototype PRs informed the final API design.
repositories.contributors:
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) opened and merged all four PRs this week: the `Uring.Res` module ([#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)), the `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` bug fix ([#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132)), the ZFS documentation warning ([#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133)), and the test hang fix ([#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134)); also closed issues [#55](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/55), [#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113), and [#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) and provided detailed review commentary on the alternative approaches in [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130)
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) authored the two earlier prototype PRs [#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and [#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) that directly shaped the final `Uring.Res` design, including exploration of encoding type information in job IDs and the suggestion to store types on the OCaml side to avoid allocation
- [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal) suggested adding a ZFS warning to the `write_fixed` documentation, which led to [#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133)

repositories.brief_summary: Week 20 saw merges adding environment override support, a Uring.Res API update, and documentation cleanup.
repositories.new_features_summary: A new Eio_unix.Stdenv.override function was added allowing selective replacement of environment resources.
repositories.new_features:
- [#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823) **Eio_unix.Stdenv.override** added by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) and refined by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5): a new `override` function in `Eio_unix.Stdenv` allows replacing individual capabilities (stdin, stdout, net, process_mgr, clock, fs, etc.) in an existing environment, making it easier to wrap or mock resources without reconstructing a full environment.
- [#832](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/832) **Simplify custom env type example** merged by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog): removed redundant `as 'a` from the custom environment type constraint example in the README.
repositories.activity_summary: Three PRs were merged this week covering a new environment override API, a compatibility fix for the Uring library, and a documentation cleanup.
repositories.activity:
- [#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823) Merged: **Add Eio_unix.Stdenv.override for updating environments** by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris), reviewed and rebased by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5), adding 41 lines across `eio_unix.ml` and `eio_unix.mli`.
- [#839](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/839) Merged: **Update for new Uring.Res API** by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5): casts `Uring.Res.t` return values back to `int` to maintain compatibility with both old and new versions of `ocaml-uring` (see [ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)).
- [#832](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/832) Merged: **Simplify custom env type in example** by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog): minor README cleanup removing the redundant `as 'a` from a type constraint.
- [#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836) Closed (not merged): **Make Linux and Posix eventfd functions configurable** by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris): closed after discussion with [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) who suggested using `dup` to reserve FDs before initialising Eio as an alternative approach.
repositories.discussions_summary: Discussion on making eventfd creation configurable concluded with a simpler workaround, and a tempdir proposal attracted commentary on atomic file rename use cases.
repositories.notable_discussions:
- [#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836) **Configurable eventfd for shell FD management**: [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) proposed making eventfd creation configurable in the Linux/Posix backends for use in `msh` (a POSIX shell). [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggested the API change wasn't needed and that opening `/dev/null`, duping it to reserve high FD numbers before Eio initialises, then closing them is a cleaner workaround.
- [#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831) **Tempdir utilities proposal**: [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) commented on the open proposal by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) noting an important use case: creating a temp file in the same directory as an existing `Eio.Path` to allow atomic cross-directory-safe renames when downloading files.
- [#838](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/838) **Eio.Promise.map** closed: the feature request for a switchless `Promise.map` function was closed (likely resolved or deferred), having been opened by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris).
repositories.trends_summary: The capability-passing environment pattern in Eio is maturing, with new tooling to compose and override environments without full reconstruction.
repositories.emerging_trends:
- The `Eio_unix.Stdenv.override` addition reflects a growing pattern in the ecosystem where programs define a custom environment type as a subtype of `Eio_unix.Stdenv.t` and need ergonomic ways to swap out individual capabilities (e.g. wrapping a process manager for debugging).
- Compatibility work with upstream `ocaml-uring` continues as that library evolves its API (e.g. introducing `Uring.Res.t`), requiring corresponding updates in Eio's Linux backend.
- Interest in shell-related use cases for Eio (FD management, temp files, process spawning) is growing, driven by projects like `msh` that are building POSIX shells on top of Eio.
repositories.issues_summary: One open issue with a tempdir utilities proposal remains active, with community input on atomic rename use cases.
repositories.contributors_summary: Three contributors were active this week: Patrick Ferris, Thomas Leonard, and Volker Diels-Grabsch.
repositories.contributors:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris): authored [#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823) (Stdenv.override) and [#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836) (configurable eventfd).
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5): authored [#839](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/839) (Uring.Res compatibility), reviewed and rebased [#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823), and provided guidance on [#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836).
- [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog): authored [#832](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/832) (README cleanup) and maintains the open [#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831) tempdir proposal.
- [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm): contributed to discussion on [#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831) with an atomic rename use case for temp files.

group_summaries.brief_summary: opam-repository saw major infrastructure releases (odoc 3.2.1, lwt 5.10.0, ocluster 0.4.0) while ocaml.org received documentation fixes.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New packages pyro-caml (Pyroscope profiler), ecma-regex, symphony-orchestrator-tui; Tour of OCaml REPL formatting fixed.
group_summaries.new_features:
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29901](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29901): Added `pyro-caml.1.0.0`, `pyro-caml-instruments.1.0.0`, and `pyro-caml-ppx.1.0.0` — a continuous OCaml profiler with Pyroscope backend and PPX instrumenter, by [Semgrep](https://github.com/semgrep)

- [ocaml/opam-repository#29910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29910): Added `symphony-orchestrator-tui.0.1.0`, a terminal UI toolkit with buffered renderer and flexbox layout, by [Matheus B. Barni](https://github.com/MatheusBBarni)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29914): Added `openrouter_api.0.1.0`, an Async-based OCaml client for the OpenRouter unified LLM API, by [mt_caret](https://github.com/mt-caret)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29830](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29830): `rocq-stdlib.9.1.0` moved from the Rocq-specific opam repo into the main opam-repository, by [Matthieu Sozeau](https://github.com/mattam82)
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29905](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29905): Added `agentxm-example-tinyflags.0.1.0`, a small OCaml feature-flag library, by [Craig Smitham](https://github.com/craigsmitham)
- [ocaml/ocaml.org#3649](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3649): Fixed the Tour of OCaml docstring code block to include the `#` REPL prompt prefix, reducing beginner confusion, by [Anatole](https://github.com/a2br)
- [ocaml/ocaml.org#3648](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3648), [#3651](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3651), [#3655](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3655), [#3657](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3657): Added 7 new OCaml Planet blog posts and 2 YouTube videos via automated scraper
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository) had an active week with 4 major infrastructure releases merged and 6+ new packages submitted
- [ocaml/ocaml.org](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org) saw content maintenance: changelog date corrections, Tour of OCaml formatting, and planet feed updates
- OCaml 5.5 compatibility is a cross-cutting theme, with opam-repository packages gaining 5.5 support and ocaml.org's platform changelogs PR accumulating 5.5-era tool releases
- Windows platform improvements are ongoing, with [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) driving two separate PRs ([ocaml/opam-repository#29907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29907), [ocaml/opam-repository#29912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29912)) covering dependency graph correctness and CI tweaks
- New AI/LLM tooling packages (`openrouter_api`, `pyro-caml`) reflect growing OCaml usage in AI infrastructure
group_summaries.activity_summary: Infrastructure merges include odoc 3.2.1, lwt 5.10.0, ocluster 0.4.0, obuilder 0.7.0; platform changelog and job listing PRs remain open on ocaml.org.

group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **odoc releases**: `odoc.3.2.1` was merged into [ocaml/opam-repository#29891](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29891) this week; the ocaml.org platform changelogs PR ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)) also covers `odoc 3.2.0`, indicating a closely tracked release cycle between the two repositories
- **merlin/ocaml-lsp**: Changelog date corrections in [ocaml/ocaml.org#3653](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3653) correspond to the same merlin 5.7.0-504 and ocaml-lsp 1.26.0 releases that flow through [ocaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository), highlighting the dependency between package publication and documentation
- **OCaml 5.5.0~beta1**: Referenced in the ocaml.org platform changelogs ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)) while opam-repository saw multiple packages — `lintcstubs-arity`, `camlp5`, `dune 3.23.1` — explicitly adding OCaml 5.5 compatibility in [ocaml/opam-repository#29904](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29904), [ocaml/opam-repository#29913](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29913), [ocaml/opam-repository#29903](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29903)
- **dune**: `dune 3.23.1` is under review in [ocaml/opam-repository#29903](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29903) while `dune-release 2.2.1` and other dune-adjacent tool changelogs are queued in the ocaml.org platform releases PR ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/opam-repository#29907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29907): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) submitted a large PR fixing incorrect `{post}` dependencies in ocaml-env packages, moving mingw-w64-shims into ocaml-env-mingw, and introducing an `x-revision` field for packages updated without version bumps

- [ocaml/opam-repository#29881](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/issues/29881): [Reynir Björnsson](https://github.com/reynir) proposed archiving `ocaml-src.{4.13,4.14,5.0}.dev` packages that lack checksums and carry `avoid-version` flags, with support from [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) and [Marcello Seri](https://github.com/mseri)
