- [ocaml/ocaml.org#3652](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3652): [Juneyoung Lee](https://github.com/aqjune) proposed listing AWS as an industrial OCaml user, citing the s2n-bignum verified cryptography project and HOL Light proofs
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OCaml 5.5 ecosystem adoption**: Multiple packages this week ([ocaml/opam-repository#29904](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29904), [ocaml/opam-repository#29913](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29913), [ocaml/opam-repository#29903](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29903)) are explicitly adding OCaml 5.5 support while ocaml.org tracks 5.5.0~beta1 in its platform changelogs ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641)), reflecting broad ecosystem adaptation
- **Windows platform investment**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) drove two PRs this week ([ocaml/opam-repository#29907](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29907), [ocaml/opam-repository#29912](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29912)) alongside QEMU and HCS backends in ocluster/obuilder ([ocaml/opam-repository#29900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29900), [ocaml/opam-repository#29887](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29887)), indicating sustained infrastructure effort for Windows support
- **AI/LLM tooling in OCaml**: New packages `openrouter_api.0.1.0` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29914)) and `pyro-caml.1.0.0` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29901](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29901)) reflect growing interest in OCaml for AI infrastructure and observability
- **OCaml minimum version floor rising**: `ocaml-version.4.1.1` dropping 4.08 ([ocaml/opam-repository#29889](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29889)) and new packages requiring OCaml 4.13+ (e.g., dune-configurator in [ocaml/opam-repository#29903](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29903)) indicate the community is converging on higher minimum versions
- **Documentation quality effort**: Both a formatting fix ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3649](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3649)) and a readability PR ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3654](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3654)) targeted the Tour of OCaml in the same week, alongside ongoing backfill of platform release changelogs ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3641](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3641))

group_summaries.brief_summary: Dune 3.24 cycle opened, odoc 3.2.1 released, js_of_ocaml gains QuickJS-NG support, and opam API removes chdir calls.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Dune 3.24 features, odoc OxCaml rendering, js_of_ocaml QuickJS-NG/DOM expansion, and opam structured path modules.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Dune 3.24 development cycle opened** with language version bump ([ocaml/dune#14559](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14559)); **allocation profiling** via `DUNE_TRACE=+alloc` ([ocaml/dune#14401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14401)); **directory targets promoted to stable** ([ocaml/dune#14579](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14579)); **conditional `(melange.modules ..)` field** added ([ocaml/dune#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14482), [ocaml/dune#14573](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14573)); **RPC client count** in watch mode status line as `[rpc N]` ([ocaml/dune#14489](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14489)) — by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) and [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro)
- **odoc OxCaml unboxed named types** now rendered with trailing `#` suffix from compiler `Pextra_ty` representation ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)) by [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w); **voodoo include-path fix** computing transitive closure of `lib_deps` before emitting `-I` flags ([ocaml/odoc#1434](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1434)) by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam)
- **opam `OpamPathName` and `OpamRepositoryPathName` modules** added to `opam-format` to eliminate hardcoded path strings ([ocaml/opam#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917)) by [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou); **`OpamSystem` commands gain `?dir` argument** replacing chdir-based APIs, removing `OpamFilename.in_dir`, `OpamSystem.in_dir`, and `OpamSystem.chdir` ([ocaml/opam#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)) by [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb)
- **js_of_ocaml QuickJS-NG runtime support** with a new `quickjs` dune profile, real-disk filesystem, and CI job running the full test suite ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo); **DOM API expansion** adding `HTMLCollection`, `Performance.now()` (closes [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#679](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/679)), and `selection*` attributes on input elements (closes [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#453](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/453)) ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221)) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon); **Wasm constant-expression globalization** reducing toplevel function size ~50% for the PRT benchmark ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211)) by [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon)
- **js_of_ocaml browser compatibility documentation** added covering Babel + core-js recipe for pre-ES2020 targets ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2216](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2216)) by [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo)
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Active week across 5 of 8 distinct repositories; [ocaml/ocamlfind](https://github.com/ocaml/ocamlfind), [ocaml/opam-file-format](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-file-format), and [ocaml-dune/spawn](https://github.com/ocaml-dune/spawn) saw no activity
- [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) opened its 3.24 development cycle with 45+ PRs merged or opened, including language version bump ([ocaml/dune#14559](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14559)), allocation profiling ([ocaml/dune#14401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14401)), and removal of the deprecated `(lang coq)` build language ([ocaml/dune#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14525))
- [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) shipped release 3.2.1 with three crash fixes for `merlin-lib` ([ocaml/odoc#1430](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1430)), `core_unix` ([ocaml/odoc#1433](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1433)), and `base` ([ocaml/odoc#1426](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1426)), and merged OxCaml unboxed named type rendering ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407))
- [ocsigen/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml) merged QuickJS-NG runtime support ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)), a large DOM API expansion ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221)), and Wasm constant-expression globalization ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211))
- [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) merged API cleanup removing chdir-based patterns ([ocaml/opam#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)) and adding structured path name modules ([ocaml/opam#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917))
- [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) had three PRs opened — locate fix ([ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)), signature-help type alias fix ([ocaml/merlin#2067](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2067)), and a structural search integration proposal ([ocaml/merlin#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064)) — with no merges to main
- [ocaml/ocaml-lsp](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp) investigated a FreeBSD 15.0 build failure in the vendored libev dependency ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605)) with a patch documented in the issue
group_summaries.activity_summary: Dune RPC hardening and performance work, odoc xref2 crash fixes, opam portability issues, and js_of_ocaml Promise/Fetch bindings opened.
group_summaries.activity:
- **ocaml/dune**: Removed deprecated `(lang coq)` Coq build language ([ocaml/dune#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14525)); fixed lock subdirectory paths ([ocaml/dune#14524](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14524)); faster file digest combining open/digest/close into one thread op ([ocaml/dune#14460](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14460)); RPC socket lifecycle hardening across fd leaks, stranded socket paths, and address reporting ([ocaml/dune#14565](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14565), [ocaml/dune#14568](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14568), [ocaml/dune#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14580)); NetBSD bootstrap fixed ([ocaml/dune#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14512)); Layer 1 of per-module lib-deps filtering merged ([ocaml/dune#14513](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14513))
- **ocaml/dune open**: `oxcaml-compiler` package recognition ([ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14544)); scheduler semaphore event wakeup replacing signal-watcher thread ([ocaml/dune#14550](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14550)); Melange 0.1 extension removal ([ocaml/dune#14574](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14574)); registry write event trace ([ocaml/dune#14536](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14536))
- **ocaml/odoc**: odoc 3.2.1 released by [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) bundling fixes for `base` breakage ([ocaml/odoc#1426](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1426)), `merlin-lib` assertion crash ([ocaml/odoc#1430](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1430)), and local module substitution regression ([ocaml/odoc#1433](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1433)); OxCaml zero alloc annotations ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) and modalities ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)) in review
- **ocaml/opam**: Shell init script exit code leak fix for Zsh/Fish ([ocaml/opam#6930](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6930)) by [Talel Zighni](https://github.com/ta2005); Solaris/Illumos terminal resize support ([ocaml/opam#6933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6933)); Windows plugin DLL loading issues filed ([ocaml/opam#6931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6931), [ocaml/opam#6932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6932)); lock file disjunction bug reported ([ocaml/opam#6935](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6935)); draft PR to prompt on auto-pinned package removal ([ocaml/opam#6923](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6923))
- **ocaml/merlin**: locate fix for let punnings ([ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)) by [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd); signature-help with type alias expansion ([ocaml/merlin#2067](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2067)) by [Carine Morel](https://github.com/lyrm); ocamlgrep structural search integration proposal updated ([ocaml/merlin#2064](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2064)) by [Martin Jambon](https://github.com/mjambon)
- **ocaml/ocaml-lsp**: FreeBSD 15.0 build failure in vendored `libev` documented with a conditional `<sys/mount.h>` patch ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605)) by [Zaraksh Rahman](https://github.com/zarakshR); upstream fix tracked at [rgrinberg/lev#101](https://github.com/rgrinberg/lev/pull/101)
- **ocsigen/js_of_ocaml**: Promise + Fetch bindings opened ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2239](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2239)); Wasm runtime small fixes ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2240](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2240)); wasm-opt removal targeting ~60% Wasm compilation time reduction ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2238](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2238)); dune 3.23 migration blocked on upstream dune issues ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)); five long-standing issues closed including JS-agnostic IR request ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#1075](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/1075))
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **dune → odoc constraint**: [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) updated the odoc version constraint in dune to `>= 3.2.0` ([ocaml/dune#14527](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14527)) to fix CI failures caused by changed markdown rendering in odoc 3.2.0, directly coupling the odoc 3.2.1 release to dune's CI health
- **js_of_ocaml → dune**: The dune 3.23 migration PR in js_of_ocaml ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2231](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2231)) is blocked on [ocaml/dune#14441](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14441) and [ocaml/dune#14438](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14438) being resolved upstream
- **OxCaml cross-repo coordination**: OxCaml-specific work spans three repositories this week — dune adds `oxcaml-compiler` toolchain recognition ([ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14544)); odoc adds unboxed named types ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)), modality rendering ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)), and zero alloc annotations ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)); js_of_ocaml updates compatibility to OxCaml 5.2.0minus31 ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225))
- **ocaml-lsp → lev**: The FreeBSD build failure in [ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605) traces to the vendored `lev` submodule maintained by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) (also dune's primary contributor this week), with an upstream fix at [rgrinberg/lev#101](https://github.com/rgrinberg/lev/pull/101)
- **Shared contributors**: [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) contributed to both [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) ([ocaml/dune#14526](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14526), [ocaml/dune#14527](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14527)) and [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), [ocaml/odoc#1436](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1436)); [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) contributed to both [ocaml/merlin](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin) ([ocaml/merlin#2066](https://github.com/ocaml/merlin/issues/2066)) and [ocaml/odoc](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc) ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422), [ocaml/odoc#1434](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1434))
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **OCaml minimum version lower bound in dune** ([ocaml/dune#14506](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14506)): debate about reverting the 4.14 minimum (introduced in 3.23.0) back to 4.11; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) argues against revert while [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) wants concrete user impact reports before deciding
- **OxCaml compiler package recognition approach** ([ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14544)): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) raised whether opam `flags: compiler` metadata should be used instead of hardcoding package names; [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) explained the current toolchain hack and roadmap to remove it once OxCaml becomes relocatable
- **`dune exec` target inference** ([ocaml/dune#14510](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14510), [ocaml/dune#14511](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14511)): [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) proposed allowing `dune exec` to infer target when unambiguous; [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) proposed directory-based disambiguation
- **opam `env_hook` PATH clobbering** ([ocaml/opam#6815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6815)): ongoing discussion about aggressive PATH overrides when custom tools are installed; [Illia Denysenko](https://github.com/juliancoffee) confirmed the issue when using a custom-built dune; proposal for direnv-style conditional PATH update
- **opam lock file disjunction for transitive deps** ([ocaml/opam#6935](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6935)): [Simmo Saan](https://github.com/sim642) reported that lock files annotate shared transitive deps of `with-doc` and `with-dev-setup` with only one flag rather than using disjunction
- **js_of_ocaml QuickJS-NG Debian packaging** ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)): [Stéphane Glondu](https://github.com/glondu) flagged that quickjs-ng FTBFS on Debian Hurd and expressed preference for keeping runtime-specific code outside jsoo itself
- **odoc zero alloc annotation implementation** ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)): [Paul-Elliot Anglès d'Auriac](https://github.com/panglesd) identified that `val_zero_alloc` is already stored in compiler cmi/cmti artefacts, prompting [Marek Kubica](https://github.com/Leonidas-from-XIV) to rewrite the implementation to read from compiler data rather than reparsing attributes
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **OxCaml toolchain integration across repos**: dune recognizing `oxcaml-compiler` ([ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14544)), odoc rendering OxCaml-specific annotations — unboxed types ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)), modalities ([ocaml/odoc#1420](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1420)), zero alloc ([ocaml/odoc#1422](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1422)) — and js_of_ocaml updating OxCaml compatibility ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2225](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2225)) indicate coordinated toolchain adoption effort
- **RPC infrastructure hardening in dune**: sustained series of socket lifecycle fixes ([ocaml/dune#14565](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14565), [ocaml/dune#14568](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14568), [ocaml/dune#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14580)) alongside the signal handling rework ([ocaml/dune#14550](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14550)) indicates a push toward production-grade RPC reliability
- **Platform portability across the toolchain**: BSD and Windows fixes span four repositories — NetBSD ([ocaml/dune#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14512)), FreeBSD ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605)), Solaris/Illumos ([ocaml/opam#6933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6933)), and Windows DLL loading ([ocaml/opam#6931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6931), [ocaml/opam#6932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6932))
- **Wasm backend performance investment in js_of_ocaml**: multiple PRs targeting both output size and JIT tier-up efficiency — constant-expression globalization ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211)), wasm-opt removal ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2238](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2238)), loop extraction ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2212](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2212))
- **Per-module incremental build improvements in dune**: [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) building a 9-layer stack ([ocaml/dune#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14492)) to narrow compile-rule dependencies to per-module library usage; Layer 1 merged ([ocaml/dune#14513](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14513)) with allocation profiling ([ocaml/dune#14401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14401)) providing measurement support
- **Broadening js_of_ocaml runtime targets**: QuickJS-NG integration ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)) extends js_of_ocaml beyond Node.js to embedded runtimes, alongside systematic DOM API expansion ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221)) and upcoming Promise/Fetch bindings ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2239](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2239))

group_summaries.brief_summary: 34 PRs merged in oxcaml/oxcaml spanning preemption, metaprogramming, DWARF debugging, and unboxed-type docs; odoc CI pin removed.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: Preemption support, ikinds enablement, magic staged modes, LLDB OxCaml plugin, and borrow_ operator docs landed in oxcaml/oxcaml.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **Initial preemption implementation** merged by [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/4881)), adding the foundational preemption mechanism to the runtime.
- **Enable ikinds** merged by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5999](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5999)), turning on integer kind support in the compiler.
- **Add `[@magic_staged_modes]`** to delay mode checks until program generation by [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6066](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6066)), enabling quoting of non-legacy (e.g. local) expressions in metaprogramming.
- **Facility to register `-X` extra params** added by [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6003](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6003)), allowing internal undocumented compiler options to be registered via a new `Extra_options` module.
- **Use new LLDB OxCaml language plugin** integrated by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4961](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/4961)), switching to a dedicated OxCaml language plugin for LLDB.
- **Bump to OxCaml LLDB 21.1.0+oxcaml0** by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5996](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5996)), picking up name-mangling-related changes for the LLDB plugin.
- **Allow setting `-gdwarf-inlined-frames` via `OCAMLPARAM`** by [Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6061](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6061)), enabling opam switches with extra debug info across all libraries.
- **Allow aliased interface files to not exist when building the cmi_bundle for metaprogramming** by [Jack Rickard](https://github.com/Skepfyr) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6043](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6043)), fixing crashes when module aliases had no CMI and preventing link-time dropping of quote-only modules.
- **Add user documentation for the `borrow_` operator** by [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5928](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5928)), providing comprehensive documentation for the borrowing feature in the uniqueness extension.
- **Merlin: add support for injected parsetrees to mpipeline** by [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6078](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6078)), enabling faster-path completion by allowing Merlin to type-check a reduced parsetree.
- **Removed temporary odoc CI pin** in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) ([oxcaml/opam-repository#45](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/45)), switching from a pre-release git commit pin to the released upstream odoc 3.2.0.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) had the most activity with 34 merged PRs from 23 contributors, covering compiler correctness, metaprogramming, DWARF/debugging, and documentation.
- [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) saw one merged PR (odoc CI pin removal) and one open issue requesting a Jane Street preview package bump to `v0.18~preview.130.100+614`.
- [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) had one in-progress draft PR ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) for the OCaml 5.4 upstream merge, currently blocked by merge conflicts.
- [oxcaml/js_of_ocaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/js_of_ocaml) and [janestreet/opam-repository](https://github.com/janestreet/opam-repository) had no recorded activity this week.
group_summaries.activity_summary: Compiler correctness fixes, Flambda2 reaper improvements, testsuite updates for OCaml 5.4, and doc expansions for unboxed types and small integers.
group_summaries.activity:
- **Compiler correctness fixes**: [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) fixed a compiler panic with `[@@unboxed]` and existentials ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6046](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6046)); [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) fixed scannable axis printing ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6060](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6060)), kind annotation checks for existential patterns ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5935](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5935)), and incorrect switch-table lifting with local coercions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6084](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6084)).
- **Metaprogramming fixes**: [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) fixed stage-tracking fatal errors hiding type errors ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6070](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6070)); [Andrej Ivašković](https://github.com/Dreian) fixed infix operator lambda generation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5982](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5982)) and constructor logic for extension constructors in quotations ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5983](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5983)).
- **Mode solver fix**: [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) corrected an unsound map comparison in the mode solver morph map ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6047](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6047)).
- **Flambda2 / Reaper improvements**: [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) fixed bugs in rewriting of sets of closures ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6005](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6005)); [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) reduced `if`-cascades in Reaper code generation ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5938](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5938)); [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) reverted a Flambda2 change that caused regressions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6085](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6085)).
- **Debugging and tooling**: [@jdimino-js](https://github.com/jdimino-js) added verbose fatal errors for array primitives ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6079](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6079)); [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) added a warning for `[@untagged]` external int8/int16 returns ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6081](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6081)); [Chris Casinghino](https://github.com/ccasin) fixed a missing-parens bug in pprintast ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6052](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6052)).
- **Testing infrastructure**: [@ttebbi-janestreet](https://github.com/ttebbi-janestreet) improved expect-asm test stability by normalising label names ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5993](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5993)); [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) added binary-level emit tests for small integers ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5942](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5942)); [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) resolved OCaml 5.4 merge conflicts and updated test baselines across four PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5972](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5972), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5988](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5988), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5989](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5989), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6015](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6015)).
- **Documentation**: [James Rayman](https://github.com/jra4) expanded small integer docs with overflow behaviour ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6069](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6069)), C interop section ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6063](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6063)), unboxed array representation table fixes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6088](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6088)), and the mixed-block v6 changelog fix ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6096](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6096)).
- **oxcaml/merlin 5.4 upstream merge**: [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) continues work on the large upstream merge draft ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) incorporating 424,854 additions across 888 files, currently in a conflicting state.
- **Jane Street preview package bump**: [Artem Pianykh](https://github.com/artempyanykh) opened [oxcaml/opam-repository#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46) requesting a bump to `v0.18~preview.130.100+614` for ~30 packages including `oxcaml_intrinsics`, `ocaml_simd`, `unboxed`, and `vec`.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **Merlin integration in oxcaml/oxcaml**: [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) merged injected parsetree support into the Merlin pipeline via the oxcaml/oxcaml tree ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6078](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6078)), while [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) opened a draft to import Merlin 5.2.0minus-39 ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6082](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6082)); separately, [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) is resolving merge conflicts in the standalone [oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239) upstream integration.
- **David Allsopp active across repositories**: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) contributed OCaml 5.4 conflict resolutions to [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5972](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5972), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5988](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5988), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5989](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5989), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6015](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6015)) and merged the odoc CI cleanup in [oxcaml/opam-repository](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository) ([oxcaml/opam-repository#45](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/45)).
- **oxcaml_intrinsics and opam packaging**: The pending package bump to `v0.18~preview.130.100+614` in [oxcaml/opam-repository#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46) includes `oxcaml_intrinsics` and `ocaml_simd`, which are used directly by [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml); the upstream motivating fix is tracked at [janestreet/ocaml_intrinsics_kernel#5](https://github.com/janestreet/ocaml_intrinsics_kernel/issues/5).
- **odoc release unblocks documentation builds**: The removal of the temporary odoc pin ([oxcaml/opam-repository#45](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/45)) means CI documentation builds across the group now use the released odoc 3.2.0 rather than a pinned pre-release commit.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Custom `[@@or_null]` payload shape design** debated on [oxcaml/oxcaml#5830](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5830) between [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js), exploring "best-kind design" vs. "ordinary-scheme design" for user-defined nullable types.
- **DWARF emission standardisation** discussed on [oxcaml/oxcaml#6093](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6093) by [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon), with [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) raising concerns about correct `DW_AT_byte_stride` and element counts for packed unboxed arrays.
- **`Shape.Uid.t` and `Global_module.Name.t` integration** discussed on [oxcaml/oxcaml#6071](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6071) by [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923), with [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) questioning potential loss of `-for-pack` information from declaration UIDs.
- **Frametables in `.rodata` section** discussed on [oxcaml/oxcaml#6094](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6094) by [Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski), with [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) providing a macOS fix for Apple's assembler not accepting `.section .rodata`.
- **Regalloc rematerialisation draft** by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6077](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6077)) reports a 5% reduction in stack slots and 2.4% reduction in spills measured across compiler files.
- **Claude-based code review skill** proposed by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) in [oxcaml/oxcaml#6072](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6072), with [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) trialling and reporting results; discussion noted pagination fixes needed.
- **Jane Street preview package bump** tracking in [oxcaml/opam-repository#46](https://github.com/oxcaml/opam-repository/issues/46) by [Artem Pianykh](https://github.com/artempyanykh), noting ~30 packages missing a release commit for `v0.18~preview.130.100+614`, with reference to upstream [janestreet/ocaml_intrinsics_kernel#5](https://github.com/janestreet/ocaml_intrinsics_kernel/issues/5).
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Metaprogramming maturity**: Multiple fixes and features landed for quotation and evaluation semantics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5982](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5982), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5983](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5983), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6043](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6043), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6066](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6066), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6070](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6070)), with larger open PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6086](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6086), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6092](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6092)) pushing new quoting capabilities.
- **Compilation speed focus**: Several open PRs labelled "compilation speed" ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6065](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6065), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6068](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6068)) alongside benchmarking-only PRs from [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6053](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6053), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6054](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6054), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6055](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6055), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6058](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6058)) measuring regalloc and prologue validation overheads.
- **DWARF and debugging investment**: Standardising DWARF emission onto exprloc attributes ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6093](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6093)) and integrating a dedicated LLDB OxCaml language plugin ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4961](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/4961)) reflect a sustained push toward native debugging support.
- **Mode polymorphism stack**: A series of draft PRs from [@ainageorges](https://github.com/ainageorges) ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5478](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5478) through [oxcaml/oxcaml#5488](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5488)) continues building toward functional mode polymorphism with level-based generalisation.
- **OCaml 5.4 integration progress**: Ongoing conflict resolution work in both [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) (via [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js)) and [oxcaml/merlin](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin) ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) reflects a coordinated push to track the upstream OCaml 5.4 release line.

group_summaries.brief_summary: GC ephemeron fixes, a new C threading API, pprintast correctness, and RFC discussion on persistent compiler processes.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New `caml_c_thread_register_in_domain` C API; pprintast package-type attribute preservation; configure argument default fix.
group_summaries.new_features:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14275](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14275) by [Jack Joergensen](https://github.com/jacknojo): **`caml_c_thread_register_in_domain`** added to the C API, enabling C threads created outside the OCaml runtime to register into a chosen domain by passing the domain's unique ID, rather than being forced into domain 0 via `caml_c_thread_register`.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14797](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14797) by [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy): **Fixed `pprintast` to preserve attributes on package types**, avoiding silent attribute loss when pretty-printing `(module M with type t = ...)` expressions; backported to the 5.5 branch.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14802](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14802) by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27): **Fixed detection of argument defaults in `configure`**, correcting a spurious error when installing OCaml via opam on OpenSUSE with the `site-config` package present (follow-up to [ocaml/ocaml#14760](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14760), [ocaml/ocaml#14761](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14761)).
group_summaries.group_overview:
- [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) saw 14 commits merged across GC ephemeron refactoring, pprintast correctness, configure fixes, stdlib documentation cleanup, and CI dependency updates.
- [ocaml/RFCs](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs) had no new commits but received a substantive design comment on the persistent compiler processes RFC ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60)).
- Active open work in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) includes a constructor auto-eta-expansion proposal ([ocaml/ocaml#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803)), `Weak` iteration additions ([ocaml/ocaml#14792](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14792)), bigarray C API exposure ([ocaml/ocaml#14804](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14804)), and an riscv64 jump-table miscompilation report ([ocaml/ocaml#14799](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14799)).
- Key contributors this week: [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) (GC work, reviews), [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) (configure, bigarray API), [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) (CI, cross-compilation), and [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) (reviews across both repositories).
group_summaries.activity_summary: GC ephemeron refactoring, cross-compilation build fixes, stdlib doc cleanup, and RFC discussion on persistent compiler processes.
group_summaries.activity:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): **Refactored ephemeron phase-change logic in `major_gc.c`**, extracting `prepare_for_ephe_sweeping` and related helpers as a behavior-neutral cleanup ahead of GC pacing work ([ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14722](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14722) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): **Fixed orphaning of ephemerons** in `runtime/major_gc.c`, addressing a bug uncovered while studying [ocaml/ocaml#14349](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14349).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808) opened by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA): **Minor build fixes for cross-compilation**, including a `.gitignore` entry for `yacc/ocamlyacc.opt` and assembly fixes for `*-pc-windows` targets with Wine or clang-cl.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14804](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14804) opened by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27): **Expose `caml_ba_compare` and related bigarray internals** currently hidden behind `CAML_INTERNALS`, to allow external code to construct `custom_operations` structs for custom bigarray wrappers.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14801](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14801) opened by [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue): **Add level to abbreviations in `Texpand`** to address a typing internals issue; under review by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14810](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14810) by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche): **Removed outdated comment from `stdlib/lazy.mli`** about ill-founded recursive definitions under `-rectypes`, superseded by [ocaml/ocaml#14805](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14805).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14811](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14811) by [Ronan Desplanques](https://github.com/ronan-d): **Fixed identifier mismatch in `Ptyp_constr` docstring** in `parsing/parsetree.mli` (`lident` → `tconstr`), reviewed by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14806](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14806) by [@vip892766gma](https://github.com/vip892766gma): **Fixed duplicated "the" typos** in `typing/typecore.ml` and `typing/errortrace_report.ml`, reviewed by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb).
- [ocaml/ocaml#14786](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14786) by [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA): **Updated GitHub Actions dependencies** (`actions/cache`, `actions/upload-artifact`, `actions/download-artifact`) in the TSan workflow; reviewed by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27).
- [ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60) by [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb): The **persistent compiler processes** RFC (open since January 2026) received a comment from [@copy](https://github.com/copy) proposing an alternative design based on exposing a cleaner compiler API by removing global state from compiler modules.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) is active in both repositories: authoring [ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60) on persistent compiler processes and reviewing [ocaml/ocaml#14811](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14811), [ocaml/ocaml#14806](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14806), [ocaml/ocaml#14792](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14792), and [ocaml/ocaml#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803) in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml).
- The discussion in [ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60) around exposing a cleaner compiler API is directly relevant to [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml) development: the proposed API cleanup would benefit both build tools (dune) and language tooling (Merlin), which in turn depends on pprintast round-trip fidelity being addressed in [ocaml/ocaml#14797](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14797) and [ocaml/ocaml#14809](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14809).
- The persistent-process RFC ([ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60)) targets the same build-tool overhead that motivates GC pacing work ([ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)) in [ocaml/ocaml](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml): both address compilation performance from different angles.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- [ocaml/ocaml#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803) by [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch): **Constructor auto-eta-expansion** — proposes auto-expanding argument-less constructors when a function type is expected (e.g. `List.map Some [1;2;3]`); [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) raised concerns about making constructor interpretation type-directed and suggested narrowing [ocaml/ocaml#9005](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/9005) to unary constructors as a less invasive alternative; a developer meeting was proposed.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14792](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14792) by [Romain Beauxis](https://github.com/toots): **`Weak.{iter, iteri, foldi_left}`** — 24 comments from [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb), [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls), and [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) debating allocation trade-offs in `foldi_left` vs. ergonomics of `iteri`.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14799](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14799) by [@nickbetteridge](https://github.com/nickbetteridge): **riscv64 jump-table miscompilation** when `-march` includes compressed instructions (e.g. `rv64gc`) — the assembler can compress 4-byte `j` slots to 2-byte `c.j`, breaking dispatch; 13 comments exploring the issue and possible fixes.
- [ocaml/ocaml#14809](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14809) by [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy): **Pretty-printing bug with `Ptyp_functor`** in OCaml 5.5 beta — inline extensions in the `Ptyp_functor` grammar are not reflected in the constructed AST, reported as a follow-on to [ocaml/ocaml#14797](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14797).
- [ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60): [@copy](https://github.com/copy) suggested that exposing a more ergonomic compiler API (removing global state from compiler modules) could be a complementary or alternative approach to persistent compiler processes, benefiting Merlin and enabling finer-grained parallelisation.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **GC ephemeron correctness**: [ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732) and [ocaml/ocaml#14722](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14722) are part of a sustained effort by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) to stabilise ephemeron handling in `major_gc.c` before the new GC pacing logic ([ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)) lands.
- **Stdlib iteration API completeness**: [ocaml/ocaml#14792](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14792) proposes `Weak.foldi_left` as a zero-allocation primitive, consistent with broader work to add fold/iter variants across stdlib collection modules.
- **Cross-compilation robustness**: [ocaml/ocaml#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808) and [ocaml/ocaml#14802](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14802) both address reliability when cross-compiling or installing on less common Linux distributions.
- **Pretty-printer round-trip fidelity**: [ocaml/ocaml#14797](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14797) and the follow-on [ocaml/ocaml#14809](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14809) highlight continued attention to `pprintast` accuracy, important for ppx rewriters and Merlin.
- **Compilation overhead reduction**: Debate in [ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60) between a persistent-process model and a cleaner compiler API reflects sustained interest in reducing per-invocation startup and `.cmi` unmarshalling costs for build tools like dune.

group_summaries.brief_summary: Activity concentrated in eio and ocaml-uring: new Uring.Res type-safe API, Stdenv.override, and CLOEXEC bug fix.
group_summaries.new_features_summary: New Uring.Res type-safe result module in ocaml-uring and Eio_unix.Stdenv.override for selective environment wrapping.
group_summaries.new_features:
- **`Uring.Res` module** added to [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) via [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131): provides a `private int` type for uring operation results, with `int_result`/`int_exn` for numeric results, `fd_result`/`fd_exn` for file descriptor results, and a `pp` pretty-printer emitting symbolic error names. Resolves the long-standing [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117) without requiring `Obj.magic` in user code.
- **`MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`** flag applied atomically when receiving file descriptors via `recvmsg` in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132), preventing FD leakage to child processes in multi-threaded programs.
- **`Eio_unix.Stdenv.override`** added to [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) via [ocaml-multicore/eio#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823): allows replacing individual capabilities (stdin, stdout, net, process_mgr, clock, fs, etc.) in an existing environment without reconstructing a full environment object.
group_summaries.group_overview:
- Activity this week was concentrated in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) (3 PRs merged) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring) (4 PRs merged); the remaining 7 repositories had no recorded activity.
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) was the primary contributor, authoring all 4 merged PRs in ocaml-uring and reviewing/rebasing the key eio PR [ocaml-multicore/eio#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823).
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) contributed the `Stdenv.override` feature [ocaml-multicore/eio#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823) and a configurable eventfd proposal [ocaml-multicore/eio#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836).
- [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s prototype PRs [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) informed the final `Uring.Res` design, though both were closed in favour of the lighter-weight approach in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131).
- Three long-standing ocaml-uring issues were closed: WSL2 `sendmsg` failure [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#55](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/55), unusable `accept` FD [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117), and ZFS `write_fixed` hang [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113).
group_summaries.activity_summary: Seven PRs merged across eio and ocaml-uring covering API safety, compatibility, bug fixes, documentation, and test reliability.
group_summaries.activity:
- [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131) Merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5): `Uring.Res` module using a `private int` type, superseding the extra-C-call approach in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) and the job-ID encoding approach in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130).
- [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132) Merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5): atomic `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` for `recvmsg`, fixing a race condition where a child process could inherit FDs before the flag was set manually.
- [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133) Merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5): `write_fixed` documentation warning for old ZFS versions, prompted by [@toastal](https://github.com/toastal), closing [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#113](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/113).
- [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134) Merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5): test hang fix on Linux 5.10, where a failed send previously caused an indefinite wait on the receive side.
- [ocaml-multicore/eio#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823) Merged: `Eio_unix.Stdenv.override` authored by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris), rebased and reviewed by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5).
- [ocaml-multicore/eio#839](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/839) Merged by [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5): eio Linux backend updated to cast `Uring.Res.t` values back to `int` for compatibility with the new ocaml-uring API.
- [ocaml-multicore/eio#832](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/832) Merged by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog): README cleanup removing a redundant `as 'a` constraint from the custom environment type example.
- [ocaml-multicore/eio#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836) Closed without merging: configurable eventfd proposal by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris); [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggested using `dup` to reserve FD numbers before Eio initialises as a simpler workaround.
group_summaries.cross_repository_work:
- **`Uring.Res` API propagation**: the new `Uring.Res` module introduced in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131) required an immediate compatibility update in [ocaml-multicore/eio](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio) via [ocaml-multicore/eio#839](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/839), demonstrating the direct dependency between the two libraries and the need for coordinated releases.
- [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) served as the integration point between both repositories this week, authoring the ocaml-uring API change and the corresponding eio adaptation in the same week.
group_summaries.notable_discussions:
- **Type-safe uring result API design** debated across three PRs in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring): [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) used an extra C call for safe int-to-FD conversion; [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) explored encoding return types in 4 bits of the job ID. [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) identified allocation overhead and Eio integration complexity (unknown error codes becoming `EUNKNOWNERR`, extra match arms per operation) as drawbacks of the fully-typed approach, favouring the `private int` approach in [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131).
- **Configurable eventfd for shell FD management** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836)): [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) proposed making eventfd creation configurable for use in `msh` (a POSIX shell built on Eio). [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) suggested that opening `/dev/null`, duping it to reserve high FD numbers before Eio initialises, then closing them achieves the same goal without an API change.
- **Tempdir utilities and atomic rename** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831)): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) noted a key use case for the open proposal by [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) — creating a temp file in the same directory as an existing `Eio.Path` to enable atomic cross-directory-safe renames when downloading files.
group_summaries.emerging_trends:
- **API safety without runtime overhead**: the `Uring.Res` module ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)) eliminates `Obj.magic` for converting uring result integers to `Unix.file_descr` using a `private int` type; the `Stdenv.override` addition ([ocaml-multicore/eio#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823)) extends the capability-passing pattern to support ergonomic composition without full environment reconstruction — both reflect a focus on making the libraries safe by default.
- **OS-compatibility hardening**: the `MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC` fix ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132)) and the Linux 5.10 test hang fix ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134)) address correctness across kernel versions and environments beyond the primary development platform.
- **Shell and systems-programming use cases**: interest in using Eio as a foundation for shell-level programs (FD management in [ocaml-multicore/eio#836](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/836), temp file handling in [ocaml-multicore/eio#831](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/831), process spawning) is growing, driven by projects like `msh`.

weekly_summary.brief_summary: Dune 3.24 cycle opened, odoc 3.2.1 released, OxCaml gained preemption support, and ocaml-uring shipped a type-safe result API.
weekly_summary.new_features_summary: New caml_c_thread_register_in_domain C API, Uring.Res type-safe module, Eio_unix.Stdenv.override, dune allocation profiling, and js_of_ocaml QuickJS-NG support.
weekly_summary.new_features:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Jack Joergensen](https://github.com/jacknojo) added **`caml_c_thread_register_in_domain`** to the C API ([ocaml/ocaml#14275](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14275)), enabling C threads to register into a chosen domain by passing its unique ID rather than always landing in domain 0.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) fixed detection of **argument defaults in `configure`** ([ocaml/ocaml#14802](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14802)), correcting a spurious error when installing OCaml via opam on OpenSUSE with `site-config` present.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [@chetmurthy](https://github.com/chetmurthy) fixed **`pprintast` to preserve attributes on package types** ([ocaml/ocaml#14797](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14797)), preventing silent attribute loss when pretty-printing `(module M with type t = ...)` expressions; backported to the 5.5 branch.
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) added the **`Uring.Res` module** ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)) providing a `private int` type for uring operation results with `int_result`/`fd_result` accessors, eliminating `Obj.magic` usage and resolving the long-standing [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117).
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) applied **`MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`** atomically when receiving file descriptors via `recvmsg` ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132)), preventing FD leakage to child processes in multi-threaded programs.
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) contributed **`Eio_unix.Stdenv.override`** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823)), allowing individual capabilities (stdin, stdout, net, fs, etc.) to be replaced in an existing environment without reconstructing a full environment object.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) merged the **initial preemption implementation** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/4881)), adding foundational preemption support to the OxCaml runtime.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) merged **ikinds enablement** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5999](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5999)), turning on integer kind support in the compiler.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) added **`[@magic_staged_modes]`** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6066](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6066)) to delay mode checks until program generation, enabling quoting of non-legacy (e.g. local) expressions in metaprogramming.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) integrated the **LLDB OxCaml language plugin** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4961](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/4961)) and bumped to **OxCaml LLDB 21.1.0+oxcaml0** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5996](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5996)) with name-mangling fixes for native debugging.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski) enabled **`-gdwarf-inlined-frames` via `OCAMLPARAM`** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6061](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6061)), providing opam-switch-wide extra debug info across all libraries.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) added **user documentation for the `borrow_` operator** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5928](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5928)), providing comprehensive documentation for the borrowing feature in the uniqueness extension.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) merged **injected parsetree support in Merlin's mpipeline** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6078](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6078)), enabling faster-path completion by type-checking a reduced parsetree.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ The **Dune 3.24 development cycle opened** with language version bump ([ocaml/dune#14559](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14559)); **allocation profiling** via `DUNE_TRACE=+alloc` ([ocaml/dune#14401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14401)); **directory targets promoted to stable** ([ocaml/dune#14579](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14579)); **conditional `(melange.modules ..)` field** added ([ocaml/dune#14482](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14482)); and **RPC client count** in watch mode status line ([ocaml/dune#14489](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14489)), by [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) and [Antonio Nuno Monteiro](https://github.com/anmonteiro).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) released **odoc 3.2.1** with three crash fixes for `merlin-lib` ([ocaml/odoc#1430](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1430)), `core_unix` ([ocaml/odoc#1433](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1433)), and `base` ([ocaml/odoc#1426](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1426)); [@art-w](https://github.com/art-w) added rendering of **OxCaml unboxed named types** with trailing `#` suffix ([ocaml/odoc#1407](https://github.com/ocaml/odoc/issues/1407)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [@hhugo](https://github.com/hhugo) added **js_of_ocaml QuickJS-NG runtime support** with a new `quickjs` dune profile and full test suite CI job ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)); [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) expanded the **DOM API** with `HTMLCollection`, `Performance.now()`, and `selection*` attributes ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2221](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2221)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [Jérôme Vouillon](https://github.com/vouillon) reduced **Wasm constant-expression globalization**, lowering toplevel function size ~50% for the PRT benchmark ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [R. Boujbel](https://github.com/rjbou) added **`OpamPathName` and `OpamRepositoryPathName` modules** to opam-format ([ocaml/opam#6917](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6917)); [Nathan Rebours](https://github.com/NathanReb) removed chdir-based APIs (`OpamFilename.in_dir`, `OpamSystem.in_dir`) in favour of a `?dir` argument ([ocaml/opam#6910](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6910)).
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Jon Ludlam](https://github.com/jonludlam) published **`odoc.3.2.1`** to opam-repository ([ocaml/opam-repository#29891](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29891)); [Raphaël Proust](https://github.com/raphael-proust) published **`lwt.5.10.0`** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29888](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29888)); [Mark Elvers](https://github.com/mtelvers) published **`ocluster.0.4.0`** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29900)) with QEMU and HCS (Windows containers) backends and **`obuilder.0.7.0`** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29887](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29887)) with Overlayfs and native Windows container support.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Semgrep](https://github.com/semgrep) added **`pyro-caml.1.0.0`**, `pyro-caml-instruments`, and `pyro-caml-ppx` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29901](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29901)), a continuous OCaml profiler with a Pyroscope backend and PPX instrumenter.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Yuriy Krasilnikov](https://github.com/YuriyKrasilnikov) added **`ecma-regex.0.1.0`** ([ocaml/opam-repository#29915](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29915)) providing ECMAScript regular expression syntax with Unicode property escapes and JSON Schema compatibility.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Anatole](https://github.com/a2br) fixed **Tour of OCaml docstring code blocks** to include the `#` REPL prompt prefix ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3649](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3649)), reducing beginner confusion.

oxcaml: -[Aspen Smith](https://github.com/glittershark) merged the **initial preemption implementation** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4881](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/4881)), adding foundational preemption support to the OxCaml runtime.
-[Liam Stevenson](https://github.com/liam923) merged **ikinds enablement** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5999](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5999)), turning on integer kind support in the compiler.

-[Jakub Bachurski](https://github.com/jbachurski) added **`[@magic_staged_modes]`** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6066](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6066)) to delay mode checks until program generation, enabling quoting of non-legacy (e.g. local) expressions in metaprogramming.

-[Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) integrated the **LLDB OxCaml language plugin** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#4961](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/4961)) and bumped to **OxCaml LLDB 21.1.0+oxcaml0** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5996](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5996)) with name-mangling fixes for native debugging.

-[Kevin Svetlitski](https://github.com/Svetlitski) enabled **`-gdwarf-inlined-frames` via `OCAMLPARAM`** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6061](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6061)), providing opam-switch-wide extra debug info across all libraries.

-[Zesen Qian](https://github.com/riaqn) added **user documentation for the `borrow_` operator** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5928](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5928)), providing comprehensive documentation for the borrowing feature in the uniqueness extension.

-[Kostia Balytskyi](https://github.com/ikostia) merged **injected parsetree support in Merlin's mpipeline** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6078](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6078)), enabling faster-path completion by type-checking a reduced parsetree.
multicore: -[Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) added the **`Uring.Res` module** ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)) providing a `private int` type for uring operation results with `int_result`/`fd_result` accessors, eliminating `Obj.magic` usage and resolving the long-standing [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#117](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/117).

-[Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) applied **`MSG_CMSG_CLOEXEC`** atomically when receiving file descriptors via `recvmsg` ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#132](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/132)), preventing FD leakage to child processes in multi-threaded programs.

-[Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) contributed **`Eio_unix.Stdenv.override`** ([ocaml-multicore/eio#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823)), allowing individual capabilities (stdin, stdout, net, fs, etc.) to be replaced in an existing environment without reconstructing a full environment object.

weekly_summary.activity:
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) refactored **ephemeron phase-change logic in `major_gc.c`** ([ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732)) and fixed **orphaning of ephemerons** ([ocaml/ocaml#14722](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14722)), part of a sustained effort ahead of GC pacing work ([ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)).
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Antonin Décimo](https://github.com/MisterDA) opened **cross-compilation build fixes** ([ocaml/ocaml#14808](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14808)) including a `.gitignore` entry for `yacc/ocamlyacc.opt` and assembly fixes for `*-pc-windows` targets; [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27) opened a PR to **expose `caml_ba_compare` and related bigarray internals** ([ocaml/ocaml#14804](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14804)) for external custom bigarray wrappers.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [Ronan Desplanques](https://github.com/ronan-d) fixed an **identifier mismatch in `Ptyp_constr` docstring** ([ocaml/ocaml#14811](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14811)); [Jacques Garrigue](https://github.com/garrigue) opened a PR to **add level to abbreviations in `Texpand`** ([ocaml/ocaml#14801](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14801)) under review by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere) fixed a **compiler panic with `[@@unboxed]` and existentials** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6046](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6046)); [Ryan Tjoa](https://github.com/rtjoa) fixed **scannable axis printing** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6060](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6060)), **kind annotation checks for existential patterns** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5935](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5935)), and **incorrect switch-table lifting with local coercions** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6084](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6084)).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [Nathanaëlle Courant](https://github.com/Ekdohibs) fixed **bugs in rewriting of sets of closures** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6005](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6005)); [Basile Clément](https://github.com/bclement-ocp) reduced `if`-cascades in **Reaper code generation** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5938](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5938)); [Mark Shinwell](https://github.com/mshinwell) reverted a Flambda2 change that caused regressions ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6085](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6085)).
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) resolved **OCaml 5.4 merge conflicts and updated test baselines** across four PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5972](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5972), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5988](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5988), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5989](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5989), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6015](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6015)); [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) continues work on the **oxcaml/merlin 5.4 upstream merge** ([oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239)) incorporating 424,854 additions across 888 files.
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) fixed a **test hang on Linux 5.10** ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#134](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/134)) where a failed send caused indefinite waiting on the receive side; added **`write_fixed` documentation warnings** for old ZFS versions ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#133](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/133)); [Volker Diels-Grabsch](https://github.com/vog) cleaned up the eio README removing a redundant type constraint example ([ocaml-multicore/eio#832](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/832)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/dune](https://github.com/ocaml/dune) removed the deprecated **`(lang coq)` Coq build language** ([ocaml/dune#14525](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14525)); hardened **RPC socket lifecycle** across fd leaks and stranded socket paths ([ocaml/dune#14565](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14565), [ocaml/dune#14568](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14568), [ocaml/dune#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14580)); fixed **NetBSD bootstrap** ([ocaml/dune#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14512)); merged Layer 1 of **per-module lib-deps filtering** ([ocaml/dune#14513](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14513)).
- __RUMINANT:tools__ [ocaml/opam](https://github.com/ocaml/opam) fixed **shell init script exit code leaks** for Zsh/Fish ([ocaml/opam#6930](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6930)); added **Solaris/Illumos terminal resize support** ([ocaml/opam#6933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6933)); filed Windows plugin DLL loading issues ([ocaml/opam#6931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6931), [ocaml/opam#6932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6932)).

weekly_summary.notable_discussions:
- __RUMINANT:core__ **Constructor auto-eta-expansion** ([ocaml/ocaml#14803](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14803)) proposed by [Alain Frisch](https://github.com/alainfrisch): auto-expanding argument-less constructors when a function type is expected (e.g. `List.map Some [1;2;3]`); [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb) raised concerns about type-directed constructor interpretation and suggested narrowing [ocaml/ocaml#9005](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/9005) to unary constructors as a less invasive alternative; a developer meeting was proposed.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **`Weak.{iter, iteri, foldi_left}` additions** ([ocaml/ocaml#14792](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14792)) attracted 24 comments from [Nicolás Ojeda Bär](https://github.com/nojb), [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche), [Stefan Muenzel](https://github.com/smuenzel), [Vincent Laviron](https://github.com/lthls), and [Daniel Bünzli](https://github.com/dbuenzli) debating allocation trade-offs in `foldi_left` vs. ergonomics of `iteri`.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **riscv64 jump-table miscompilation** ([ocaml/ocaml#14799](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14799)) when `-march` includes compressed instructions (`rv64gc`) — the assembler can compress 4-byte `j` slots to 2-byte `c.j`, breaking dispatch; 13 comments exploring the issue and possible fixes.
- __RUMINANT:core__ [@copy](https://github.com/copy) proposed on **[ocaml/RFCs#60](https://github.com/ocaml/RFCs/issues/60)** that exposing a cleaner compiler API by removing global state from compiler modules could be a complementary alternative to persistent compiler processes, benefiting Merlin and enabling finer-grained parallelisation.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Custom `[@@or_null]` payload shape design** debated on [oxcaml/oxcaml#5830](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5830) between [@julesjacobs](https://github.com/julesjacobs) and [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js), exploring "best-kind design" vs. "ordinary-scheme design" for user-defined nullable types.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Regalloc rematerialisation draft** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6077](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6077)) by [Xavier Clerc](https://github.com/xclerc) reports a 5% reduction in stack slots and 2.4% reduction in spills measured across compiler files.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Claude-based code review skill** ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6072](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6072)) proposed by [Richard Eisenberg](https://github.com/goldfirere), with [Simon Spies](https://github.com/spiessimon) trialling and reporting results; discussion noted pagination fixes needed.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OCaml minimum version lower bound in dune** ([ocaml/dune#14506](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14506)): debate about reverting the 4.14 minimum (introduced in 3.23.0) back to 4.11; [Rudi Grinberg](https://github.com/rgrinberg) argues against revert while [Shon Feder](https://github.com/shonfeder) wants concrete user impact reports before deciding.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml compiler package recognition approach** ([ocaml/dune#14544](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14544)): [Anil Madhavapeddy](https://github.com/avsm) raised whether opam `flags: compiler` metadata should be used instead of hardcoding package names; [Ali Caglayan](https://github.com/Alizter) explained the toolchain hack roadmap toward OxCaml relocatability.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **opam `env_hook` PATH clobbering** ([ocaml/opam#6815](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6815)): ongoing discussion about aggressive PATH overrides when custom tools are installed; [Illia Denysenko](https://github.com/juliancoffee) confirmed the issue with a custom-built dune; proposal for direnv-style conditional PATH updates.
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **Type-safe uring result API design** debated across three PRs: [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27)'s [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#129](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/129) used an extra C call; [ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#130](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/130) explored encoding return types in 4 bits of the job ID; [Thomas Leonard](https://github.com/talex5) favoured the `private int` approach ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)) to avoid allocation overhead and extra match arms per operation.
- __RUMINANT:ecosystem__ [Juneyoung Lee](https://github.com/aqjune) proposed **listing AWS as an industrial OCaml user** ([ocaml/ocaml.org#3652](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml.org/issues/3652)) on ocaml.org, citing the s2n-bignum verified cryptography project and HOL Light proofs.
