- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **Metaprogramming maturity**: multiple fixes and features landed for quotation and evaluation semantics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5982](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5982), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5983](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5983), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6043](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6043), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6066](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6066), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6070](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6070)) with larger open PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6086](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6086), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6092](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6092)) pushing new quoting capabilities.
- __RUMINANT:oxcaml__ **OCaml 5.4 integration into OxCaml**: ongoing conflict resolution by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) and by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) in [oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239) reflects a coordinated push to track the upstream OCaml 5.4 release line, continuing the pattern established in weeks 18-19.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **OxCaml toolchain integration across the tool ecosystem**: dune, odoc, js_of_ocaml, and Merlin all received OxCaml-specific adaptations this week, indicating coordinated toolchain adoption effort that has accelerated over the past four weeks.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Wasm backend performance investment in js_of_ocaml**: multiple PRs targeting output size and JIT efficiency — constant-expression globalization ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2211](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2211)), wasm-opt removal targeting ~60% compilation time reduction ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2238](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2238)) — alongside QuickJS-NG integration ([ocsigen/js_of_ocaml#2229](https://github.com/ocsigen/js_of_ocaml/issues/2229)) extending targets beyond Node.js.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Per-module incremental build improvements in dune**: [Robin Bate Boerop](https://github.com/robinbb) building a 9-layer stack ([ocaml/dune#14492](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14492)) to narrow compile-rule dependencies to per-module library usage; Layer 1 merged ([ocaml/dune#14513](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14513)) with allocation profiling ([ocaml/dune#14401](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14401)) providing measurement infrastructure.
- __RUMINANT:tools__ **Dune RPC infrastructure hardening**: sustained series of socket lifecycle fixes ([ocaml/dune#14565](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14565), [ocaml/dune#14568](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14568), [ocaml/dune#14580](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14580)) alongside signal handling rework ([ocaml/dune#14550](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14550)) reflects a push toward production-grade RPC reliability.
- __RUMINANT:core__ **GC ephemeron correctness investment**: [ocaml/ocaml#14732](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14732) and [ocaml/ocaml#14722](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14722) are part of sustained cleanup by [Gabriel Scherer](https://github.com/gasche) in `major_gc.c` ahead of GC pacing work ([ocaml/ocaml#14796](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml/issues/14796)).
- __RUMINANT:multicore__ **API safety without runtime overhead**: `Uring.Res` ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)) eliminates `Obj.magic` using a `private int` type; `Stdenv.override` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823)) extends capability-passing for ergonomic composition — both reflect a focus on making the libraries safe by default.

-**AI/LLM tooling in OCaml**: `openrouter_api.0.1.0` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29914](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29914)) and `pyro-caml.1.0.0` ([ocaml/opam-repository#29901](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29901)) reflect growing interest in OCaml for AI infrastructure and observability, continuing the pattern seen in week 19.

-**Platform portability hardening**: BSD and Windows fixes span four tool repositories — NetBSD ([ocaml/dune#14512](https://github.com/ocaml/dune/issues/14512)), FreeBSD ([ocaml/ocaml-lsp#1605](https://github.com/ocaml/ocaml-lsp/issues/1605)), Solaris/Illumos ([ocaml/opam#6933](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6933)), and Windows DLL loading ([ocaml/opam#6931](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6931), [ocaml/opam#6932](https://github.com/ocaml/opam/issues/6932)) alongside ocluster/obuilder QEMU and HCS backends ([ocaml/opam-repository#29900](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29900), [ocaml/opam-repository#29887](https://github.com/ocaml/opam-repository/pull/29887)).
oxcaml: -**Metaprogramming maturity**: multiple fixes and features landed for quotation and evaluation semantics ([oxcaml/oxcaml#5982](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5982), [oxcaml/oxcaml#5983](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/5983), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6043](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6043), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6066](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6066), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6070](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6070)) with larger open PRs ([oxcaml/oxcaml#6086](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6086), [oxcaml/oxcaml#6092](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml/pull/6092)) pushing new quoting capabilities.

-**OCaml 5.4 integration into OxCaml**: ongoing conflict resolution by [David Allsopp](https://github.com/dra27-js) in [oxcaml/oxcaml](https://github.com/oxcaml/oxcaml) and by [@dkalinichenko-js](https://github.com/dkalinichenko-js) in [oxcaml/merlin#239](https://github.com/oxcaml/merlin/issues/239) reflects a coordinated push to track the upstream OCaml 5.4 release line, continuing the pattern established in weeks 18-19.
multicore: -**API safety without runtime overhead**: `Uring.Res` ([ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring#131](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/ocaml-uring/issues/131)) eliminates `Obj.magic` using a `private int` type; `Stdenv.override` ([ocaml-multicore/eio#823](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/eio/issues/823)) extends capability-passing for ergonomic composition — both reflect a focus on making the libraries safe by default.
